1.1: SINCE many have desired to have in writing the story of those works, with which we are familiar,
1.2: According to what was handed down to us by those who from the beginning were eyewitnesses and ministers of that very word,
1.3: And since these were seen by me also because I was near and considered them all very carefully, I will therefore write to you everything in its order, most honorable Theophilus,
1.4: So that you may know the truth of the words, by which you were made a convert.
1.5: There was in the days of Herod, king of Judaea, a priest whose name was Zacharias, of the order of ministry of the house of Abijah; and his wife was of the daughters of Aaron, and her name was Elizabeth.
1.6: They were both righteous before God, and walked in all his commandments, and in the righteousness of the Lord without blame.
1.7: But they had no son, because Elizabeth was barren, and they were both well on in years.
1.8: And it happened, while he was ministering in the order of his ministry before God,
1.9: According to the custom of the priesthood, his turn came to burn incense; so he entered the temple of the Lord.
1.10: And all the congregation of the people prayed outside, at the time of incense.
1.11: And the angel of the Lord appeared to Zacharias, standing on the right of the altar of incense.
1.12: And when Zacharias saw him he became dumbfounded, and fear came upon him.
1.13: And the angel said to him, Fear not, Zacharias; for your prayer has been heard, and your wife Elizabeth will bear you a son, and you will call his name John.
1.14: And you will have joy and gladness; and a great many will rejoice at his birth.
1.15: For he will be great before the Lord, and he will not drink wine and strong drink; and he will be filled with the Holy Spirit, while he is still in the womb of his mother.
1.16: And many Israelites he will cause to turn to the Lord their God.
1.17: And he will go before them with the spirit and the power of Elijah, to turn the hearts of parents to their children, and those who are disobedient to the wisdom of the righteous; and he will prepare a true people for the Lord.
1.18: And Zacharias said to the angel, How will I understand this? for I am an old man, and my wife is well on in years.
1.19: And the angel answered, saying to him, I am Gabriel, who stand in the presence of God; and I am sent to speak to you, and to bring you these glad tidings.
1.20: From henceforth you will be dumb, and not able to speak, till the day these things happen, because you did not believe these my words which are to be fulfilled in their time.
1.21: Now the people stood waiting for Zacharias, and wondered because he remained so long in the temple.
1.22: When Zacharias came out, he could not speak with them; and they understood that he had seen a vision in the temple; and he made signs to them with his eyes, but remained dumb.
1.23: And when the days of his ministry were finished, he went to his house.
1.24: And it happened after those days, his wife Elizabeth conceived, and hid herself for five months; and she said,
1.25: The Lord has done these things to me in the days that he has been mindful of me, to remove my reproach among men.
1.26: Now in the sixth month the angel Gabriel was sent from God to Galilee, to a city called Nazareth,
1.27: To a virgin who was acquired for a price for a man named Joseph, of the house of David; and the name of the virgin was Mary.
1.28: And the angel went in and said to her, Peace be to you, O full of grace; our Lord is with you, O blessed one among women.
1.29: When she saw him, she was disturbed at his word, and wondered what kind of salutation this could be.
1.30: And the angel said to her, Fear not, Mary; for you have found grace with God.
1.31: For behold, you will conceive and give birth to a son, and you will call his name Jesus.
1.32: He will be great, and he will be called the Son of the Highest; and the Lord God will give him the throne of his father David.
1.33: And he will rule over the house of Jacob for ever; and there will be no limit to his kingdom.
1.34: Then Mary said to the angel, How can this be, for no man has known me.
1.35: The angel answered and said to her, The Holy Spirit will come, and the power of the Highest will rest upon you; therefore the one who is to be born of you is holy, and he will be called the Son of God.
1.36: And behold, Elizabeth your kinswoman has also conceived a son in her old age; and yet this is the sixth month with her, who is called barren.
1.37: For nothing is impossible for God.
1.38: Mary said, Here I am, a handmaid of the Lord; let it be to me according to your word. And the angel went away from her.
1.39: In those days, Mary rose up, and went hurriedly to a mountain, to a city of Judaea.
1.40: And she entered the house of Zacharias, and saluted Elizabeth.
1.41: And when Elizabeth heard the salutation of Mary, the babe leaped in her womb; and Elizabeth was filled with the Holy Spirit.
1.42: And she cried in a loud voice, and said to Mary, Blessed are you among women, and blessed is the fruit of your womb.
1.43: How does this happen to me, that the mother of my Lord should come to me?
1.44: For behold, when the voice of your salutation fell on my ears, the babe in my womb leaped with great joy.
1.45: And blessed is she who believed; for there will be a fulfillment of the things which were spoken to her from the Lord.
1.46: And Mary said, My soul magnifies the Lord,
1.47: And my spirit rejoices in God my Saviour.
1.48: For he has regarded the meekness of his handmaid; for behold, from henceforth, all generations shall envy me.
1.49: For he who is mighty has done great things to me; holy is his name.
1.50: And his mercy is for centuries and generations, upon those who fear him.
1.51: He has brought victory with his arm; he has scattered the proud in the imagination of their heart.
1.52: He has put down the mighty from their seats, and he has lifted up the meek.
1.53: He has filled the hungry with good things; and dismissed the rich empty.
1.54: He has helped his servant Israel, and has remembered his mercy,
1.55: Just as he spoke with our forefathers, with Abraham, and with his seed for ever.
1.56: Mary stayed with Elizabeth about three months, and then returned to her own home.
1.57: Now the time came for Elizabeth to be delivered, and she gave birth to a son.
1.58: And when her neighbors and relatives heard that God had increased his mercy to her, they rejoiced with her.
1.59: And it happened on the eighth day, they came to circumcise the boy; and they would have called him Zacharias, after the name of his father.
1.60: And his mother answered and said to them, Not so; but he should be called John.
1.61: And they said to her, There is no man in your family, who is called by this name.
1.62: Then they made signs to his father, what he wanted to call him.
1.63: And he asked for a tablet and wrote, saying, John is his name. And every one was surprised.
1.64: And immediately his mouth and his tongue were opened, and he spoke and blessed God.
1.65: And fear came on all their neighbors; and these things were spoken throughout the mountain of Judaea.
1.66: And all who heard it reasoned in their hearts, saying, What a boy he will be! And the hand of the Lord was with him.
1.67: And his father Zacharias was filled with the Holy Spirit, and prophesied, and said,
1.68: Blessed is the Lord, the God of Israel; for he has visited his people and wrought a salvation for them.
1.69: And he has raised up a horn of salvation for us in the house of his servant David;
1.70: Just as he spoke by the mouth of his holy prophets who have been for ages,
1.71: That he would save us from our enemies, and from the hand of all who hate us.
1.72: He has shown mercy to our fathers, and he has remembered his holy covenants;
1.73: And the oaths which he swore to Abraham our father,
1.74: To grant to us, that we may be saved from the hand of our enemies, and serve before him without fear,
1.75: In justice and righteousness all our days.
1.76: And you, boy, will be called the prophet of the Highest; for you will go before the face of the Lord, to prepare his way;
1.77: To give knowledge of life to his people by the forgiveness of their sins,
1.78: Through the mercy and kindness of our God; whereby we shall be visited by a ray from above,
1.79: To give light to those who sit in darkness and in the shadow of death, to guide our feet into the way of peace.
1.80: The boy grew and became strong in spirit; and he was in the desert until the day of his appearance to Israel.

2.1: AND it happened in those days that there went out a decree from Caesar Augustus, to take a census of all the people in his empire.
2.2: This first census took place during the governorship of Quirinius in Syria.
2.3: And every man went to be registered in his own city.
2.4: Joseph also went up from Nazareth, a city of Galilee, to Judaea, to the city of David, which is called Bethlehem; because he was of the house and family of David;
2.5: With his acquired wife Mary, while she was with child, that they might be registered there.
2.6: And it came to pass while they were there, that her days of deliverance were to be fulfilled.
2.7: And she gave birth to her first-born son; and she wrapped him in swaddling clothes, and laid him in a manger; because they had no place where they were lodging.
2.8: Now there were shepherds in that region, where they were staying, and they were watching their flocks at night.
2.9: And behold, the angel of God came to them, and the glory of the Lord shone on them; and they were seized with a great fear.
2.10: And the angel said to them, Do not be afraid; for behold, I bring you glad tidings of great joy, which will be to all the world.
2.11: For today is born to you in the city of David, a Saviour, who is the Lord Christ.
2.12: And this is a sign for you; You will find a babe wrapped in swaddling clothes, and laid in a manger.
2.13: And suddenly there appeared with the angel, many hosts of heaven, praising God, and saying,
2.14: Glory to God in the highest, and on earth peace and good hope for men.
2.15: And it happened, when the angels departed from them and went to heaven, the shepherds spoke to one another, saying, Let us go to Bethlehem, and see this thing that has happened, as the Lord has shown to us.
2.16: And they came very hurriedly, and found Mary, and Joseph, and the babe laid in the manger.
2.17: When they saw it, they made known the word which was spoken to them concerning the boy.
2.18: And all who heard it were amazed at the things which were spoken by the shepherds.
2.19: But Mary treasured all these things, and dwelt on them in her heart.
2.20: And the shepherds returned, glorifying and praising God for all that they had seen and heard, as it was spoken to them.
2.21: And when eight days were fulfilled to circumcise the child, his name was called Jesus; because he was named by the angel before he was conceived in the womb.
2.22: And when the days for their purification were fulfilled, according to the law of Moses, they brought him up to Jerusalem, to present him to the Lord;
2.23: As it is written in the law of the Lord, Every male that opens the womb shall be called holy to the Lord;
2.24: And to offer a sacrifice, as it is said in the law of the Lord, A pair of turtledoves, or two young pigeons.
2.25: Now there was a man in Jerusalem, whose name was Simon; and this man was pious and righteous, waiting for the consolation of Israel; and the Holy Spirit was upon him.
2.26: And it was said to him by the Holy Spirit, that he would not see death, until he sees the Anointed of the Lord.
2.27: This man was led by the Spirit to the temple; and when the parents brought in the boy Jesus, to do for him according to what is commanded in the law,
2.28: He received him in his arms, and blessed God, and said,
2.29: Now dismiss thy servant, O my Lord, in peace, according to your word;
2.30: For behold, mine eyes have already seen your mercies,
2.31: Which you have prepared before the face of all peoples,
2.32: A light for a revelation to the Gentiles, and a glory to your people Israel.
2.33: And Joseph and his mother marvelled about these things which were spoken concerning him.
2.34: And Simon blessed them, and he said to Mary, his mother, Behold, this one is appointed for the fall and for the rise of many in Israel, and for a sign of dispute;
2.35: And a sword will pierce through your own soul; so that the thoughts of the hearts of many may be revealed.
2.36: And Hannah the prophetess, the daughter of Phanuel, of the tribe of Asher, was of a great age; and she had lived seven years with her husband from the days of her virginity.
2.37: Then she became a widow for about eighty-four years, and she never left the temple, and with fasting and prayer she worshipped day and night.
2.38: She also stood up at that hour, and gave thanks to the Lord, and spoke concerning him to every man who was looking forward to the salvation of Jerusalem.
2.39: And when they had done everything according to the law of the Lord, they returned to Galilee, to their own city Nazareth.
2.40: The boy grew and became strong in spirit, filled with wisdom; and the grace of God was upon him.
2.41: And his people went every year to Jerusalem during the feast of the passover.
2.42: And when he was twelve years old, they went up to the feast, as they were accustomed.
2.43: And when the feast days were over, they returned; but the boy Jesus remained in Jerusalem; and Joseph and his mother did not know it.
2.44: They thought that he was with the children of their party; and when they went a day’s journey, they sought for him among their own people and those who knew them.
2.45: But they could not find him; so they returned again to Jerusalem, looking for him.
2.46: After three days, they found him in the temple, sitting in the midst of the teachers, listening to them, and asking them questions.
2.47: And all those who heard him were amazed at his wisdom and his answers.
2.48: And when they saw him, they were astonished; and his mother said to him, My son, why have you done so to us? behold, I and your father have been looking for you with much anxiety.
2.49: He said to them, Why were you looking for me? did you not know that I would be in the house of my Father?
2.50: But they could not understand the word which he said to them.
2.51: So he went down with them and came to Nazareth; and he was subject unto them. And his mother treasured all these words in her heart.
2.52: And Jesus grew in his stature and in his wisdom, and in favor with God and men.

3.1: IN the fifteenth year of the reign of Tiberius Caesar, during the governorship of Pontius Pilate in Judaea, when Herod was tetrarch of Galilee, and his brother Philip tetrarch of Ituraea and of the region of Trachonitis, and Lysanius tetrarch of Abilene,
3.2: During the high priesthood of Annas and Caiaphas, the word of God came to John, son of Zacharias, in the wilderness.
3.3: And he came throughout the country around Jordan, preaching the baptism of repentance for the forgiveness of sins;
3.4: As it is written in the book of the words of Isaiah the prophet, who said, The voice which calls in the wilderness, Prepare the way of the Lord, make the paths of our God straight in the plain.
3.5: Let all the valleys be filled up, and all the mountains and hills be levelled; let the crooked places be made straight, and the rough places like a plain;
3.6: And let every flesh see the salvation of God.
3.7: And he said to the people, who were coming to him to be baptized, O offspring of scorpions, who has warned you to escape from the anger which is coming?
3.8: Therefore bring forth fruits which are worthy of repentance; and do not begin to say within yourselves, We have Abraham as our father; for I say to you that God can raise up children for Abraham from these stones.
3.9: Behold, the axe is already placed at the root of the trees; therefore every tree which bears not good fruits will be cut down and dropped in the fire.
3.10: And the people asked him saying, What then shall we do?
3.11: He answered and said to them, He who has two shirts, let him give to him who has not; and he who has food, let him do likewise.
3.12: And there came also publicans to be baptized, and they said to him, Teacher, what shall we do?
3.13: He said to them, Do not exact anything more over what is commanded you to exact.
3.14: And the soldiers also asked him saying, What shall we do? And he said to them, Do not molest any man, and do not despise any man; your own wages should be enough for you.
3.15: While the people were placing their hope on John, and all of them were thinking in their hearts, that perhaps he is the Christ;
3.16: John answered and said to them, Behold, I baptize you with water; but one is coming after me, who is greater than I, the strings of whose shoes I am not worthy to untie; he will baptize you with the Holy Spirit and with fire;
3.17: He holds a shovel in his hand, and purifies his threshing; the wheat he gathers into his barns, and the straw he burns in the unquenchable fire.
3.18: Many other things also, he taught and preached to the people.
3.19: Now Herod the tetrarch, because he was rebuked by John concerning Herodias wife of Philip his brother, and for all the evil things that he was doing,
3.20: Added this also to them all, that he put John into prison.
3.21: It came to pass when all the people were baptized, Jesus also was baptized, and while he prayed the heaven was opened,
3.22: And the Holy Spirit descended on him, like a dove, and a voice from heaven, saying, You are my beloved Son, with whom I am pleased.
3.23: Now Jesus was about thirty years old, and he was supposed to be the son of Joseph, the son of Heli,
3.24: The son of Matthat, the son of Levi, the son of Melchi, the son of Jannai, the son of Joseph,
3.25: The son of Mattathias, the son of Amos, the son of Nahum, the son of Esli, the son of Naggai,
3.26: The son of Maath, the son of Mattathias, the son of Semei, the son of Joseph, the son of Juda,
3.27: The son of John, the son of Rhesa, the son of Zerubbabel, the son of Shealtiel, the son of Neri,
3.28: The son of Melchi, the son of Addi, the son of Kosam, the son of Elmodad, the son of Er,
3.29: The son of Jose, the son of Eliezer, the son of Jorim, the son of Mattitha, the son of Levi,
3.30: The son of Simon, the son of Juda, the son of Joseph, the son of Jonan, the son of Eliakim,
3.31: The son of Melea, the son of Mani, the son of Matta, the son of Nathan, the son of David,
3.32: The son of Jesse, the son of Obed, the son of Boaz, the son of Salmon, the son of Nahshon,
3.33: The son of Aminadab, the son of Aram, the son of Hezron, the son of Perez, the son of Juda,
3.34: The son of Jacob, the son of Isaac, the son of Abraham, the son of Terah, the son of Nahor,
3.35: The son of Serug, the son of Arau, the son of Peleg, the son of Eber, the son of Shalah,
3.36: The son of Cainan, the son of Arphaxad, the son of Shem, the son of Noah, the son of Lamech,
3.37: The son of Methuselah, the son of Enoch, the son of Jared, the son of Mahalaleel, the son of Cainan,
3.38: The son of Enosh, the son of Seth, the son of Adam, who was of God.

4.1: NOW Jesus, full of the Holy Spirit, returned from the Jordan, and the Spirit carried him away into the wilderness,
4.2: Forty days, in order that he might be tempted by the adversary. And he did not eat anything in those days; and when they were over, at last he became hungry.
4.3: And the adversary said to him, If you are the Son of God, command this stone to become bread.
4.4: Jesus answered and said to him, It is written, That it is not by bread alone that man can live, but by every word of God.
4.5: Then Satan took him up to a high mountain, and showed him all the kingdoms of the earth in a short time.
4.6: And the adversary said to him, I will give you all this power and its glory, which are entrusted to me, and I give it to whom I please;
4.7: If therefore you worship me, it will all be yours.
4.8: Jesus answered and said to him, It is written, You shall worship the Lord your God, and him only you shall serve.
4.9: And he brought him to Jerusalem and made him to stand up on the pinnacle of the temple, and said to him, If you are the Son of God, throw yourself down from here;
4.10: For it is written, That he will command his angels concerning you, to watch you;
4.11: And they will take you up in their arms, so that even your foot may not strike a stone.
4.12: Jesus answered and said to him, It is said, You shall not tempt the Lord your God.
4.13: When the adversary was through with all his temptations, he left him for some time.
4.14: So Jesus returned in the power of the Spirit to Galilee; and the fame about him went out through all the country around them.
4.15: And he taught in their synagogues, and was praised by every man.
4.16: And he came to Nazareth, where he had been brought up; and he entered the synagogue on the sabbath day, as was the custom, and stood up to read.
4.17: And the book of the prophet Isaiah was given to him. And Jesus opened the book, and found the place where it is written,
4.18: The Spirit of the Lord is upon me; because of this he has anointed me, to preach good tidings to the poor; and he has sent me to heal the broken-hearted, and to proclaim release to the captives, and sight to the blind; to strengthen with forgiveness those who are bruised
4.19: And to preach the acceptable year of the Lord.
4.20: And he rolled up the scroll and gave it to the attendant, and went and sat down. And the eyes of all who were in the synagogue were fixed on him.
4.21: And he began to say to them, To-day this scripture is fulfilled in your ears.
4.22: And all testified to him, and were amazed by the words of grace which came out of his mouth. And they said, Is not this man the son of Joseph?
4.23: Jesus said to them, You might probably tell me this proverb, ôPhysician, heal yourselfö; and all that we heard you did in Capernaum, do also here in your own city.
4.24: Then he said, Truly I say to you, No prophet is acceptable in his own city.
4.25: For truly I say to you, There were many widows in Israel in the days of the prophet Elijah, when the heaven was closed for three years and six months, and there was a great famine throughout the land;
4.26: Yet Elijah was not sent to one of them, but to Zarephath of Sidon, to a widow.
4.27: And there were many lepers in Israel in the days of the prophet Elisha, and yet not one of them was cleansed, except Naaman the Syrian.
4.28: When those who were in the synagogue heard these things, they were all filled with anger.
4.29: And they rose up, and took him outside the city, and brought him to the brow of the mountain, on which their city was built, that they might throw him down from a cliff.
4.30: But he passed through the midst of them and went away.
4.31: And he went down to Capernaum, a city of Galilee, and he taught them on the sabbaths.
4.32: And they were astonished at his teaching; because his word had power.
4.33: And there was in the synagogue a man who had an unclean, demonic spirit, and he cried in a loud voice,
4.34: And said, Leave me alone, what have we in common, O Jesus the Nazarene? have you come to destroy us? I know who you are, ôHoly One of Godö!
4.35: And Jesus rebuked him, and said, Keep quiet, and come out of him. The demon threw him in the midst, and went out of him, and did him no harm.
4.36: And every man was seized with amazement, and spoke among themselves, saying, What kind of word is this, that he commands unclean spirits with authority and power, and they go out!
4.37: And the fame about him went out through all the country around them.
4.38: And when Jesus left the synagogue, he entered the house of Simon. And Simon’s mother-in-law was suffering with a severe fever; and they besought him for her.
4.39: And he stood by her, and rebuked the fever, and it left her; and she rose up immediately and ministered to them.
4.40: When the sun was setting, all who had sick people suffering from divers diseases brought them to him; and he laid his hand on each one of them, and healed them.
4.41: Demons also came out of many, who cried out saying, You are the Christ, the Son of God. And he rebuked them, and he would not allow them to speak; that they might not know that he was the Christ.
4.42: And in the morning, he came out and went to a desert place; and the people were looking for him, and came where he was; and they held him so that he might not leave them.
4.43: But Jesus said to them, I must preach the kingdom of God in other cities also; because I was sent for this.
4.44: And he preached in the synagogues of Galilee.

5.1: IT came to pass when the people gath ered around him to hear the word of God, he stood on the shore of the lake of Gennesaret.
5.2: And he saw two boats standing by the lake; but the fishermen had got out of them, and were washing their nets.
5.3: One of them belonged to Simon Peter; so Jesus went up and sat in it, and he asked to row it a little way from the shore to the water. And he sat and taught the people from the boat.
5.4: When he was through speaking, he said to Simon, Row out to the deep, and cast your net for a catch.
5.5: Simon answered and said to him, Teacher, we have toiled all night, and have caught nothing; but just because of your word, I will cast the net.
5.6: And when they had done this, they inclosed a great many fish; and their net was breaking.
5.7: So they signalled to their partners in the other boat, to come and help them. And when they came, they filled both the boats, till they were almost sinking.
5.8: When Simon Peter saw it, he fell at the feet of Jesus, and said to him, I beg you, my Lord, leave me alone, for I am a sinful man.
5.9: For he was amazed, and all who were with him, because of the catch of fish which they took.
5.10: So also was it with James and John, sons of Zebedee, who were partners with Simon. But Jesus said to Simon, Do not be afraid; from henceforth you will be catching men for life.
5.11: And they brought the boats to land, and left everything and followed him.
5.12: When Jesus was in one of the cities, there came a man who was covered with leprosy; and he saw Jesus and fell on his face, and besought him, and said, My Lord, if you will, you can cleanse me.
5.13: And Jesus stretched out his hand and touched him, and said to him, I will, be clean; and immediately his leprosy left him.
5.14: And he charged him not to tell any man; but go and show yourself to the priests, and make an offering for your cleansing, as Moses commanded, for a testimony to them.
5.15: And the fame concerning him went out the more; and many people gathered to hear him, and to be healed of their diseases.
5.16: But he departed into the wilderness, and prayed.
5.17: It came to pass on one of the days when Jesus was teaching, the Pharisees and the teachers of the law were sitting, who had come from every town of Galilee and Judaea and Jerusalem. And the power of God was present to heal them.
5.18: And some men brought a paralytic on a quilt-bed; and they wanted to go in and lay him before him.
5.19: And when they found they were not able to carry him in, because of many people, they went up on the roof, and they lowered him down on his quilt-bed from the ceiling into the midst before Jesus.
5.20: When Jesus saw their faith, he said to the paralytic, Man, your sins are forgiven.
5.21: And the scribes and the Pharisees began to reason saying, Who is this man who talks blasphemy? Who can forgive sins, except God only?
5.22: But Jesus knew their thoughts, and he answered and said to them, What do you reason in your heart?
5.23: Which is easier to say, Your sins are forgiven, or just to say, Arise and walk?
5.24: But that you may know that the Son of man has authority on earth to forgive sins, he said to the paralytic, I tell you, Arise, take up your quilt-bed and go to your home.
5.25: And immediately he rose up before their eyes, and took his quilt-bed and went to his house, praising God.
5.26: And every man was seized with amazement, and they praised God, and were filled with fear, saying, To-day we have seen wonders.
5.27: After these things, Jesus went out and saw a publican named Levi, sitting at the custom house; and he said to him, Follow me.
5.28: So he left everything, and rose up, and went after him.
5.29: And Levi gave him a great reception in his house; and there was a large gathering of publicans and others, who were guests with them.
5.30: And the scribes and the Pharisees murmured and said to his disciples, Why do you eat and drink with publicans and sinners?
5.31: And Jesus answered and said to them, A physician is not needed for those who are well, but for those who are seriously sick.
5.32: I have not come to call the righteous, but the sinners to repentance.
5.33: They said to him, Why do the disciples of John always fast and pray, and also those of the Pharisees; but yours eat and drink?
5.34: He said to them, You cannot make the sons of the wedding feast fast, so long as the bridegroom is with them.
5.35: But the days will come, when the bridegroom is taken from them, then they will fast in those days.
5.36: And he told them a parable, No man cuts a piece of cloth from a new garment and puts it on a worn out garment; so that he may not cut the new, and the new piece will not blend with the old.
5.37: No man pours new wine into worn out skins; else the new wine will rend the skins, and the wine will run out, and the skins will be ruined.
5.38: But they pour new wine into new skins, and both are well preserved.
5.39: And no man drinks old wine, and immediately wants new wine; for he says, The old is delicious.

6.1: IT came to pass on the sabbath, as Jesus walked through the wheat fields, his disciples plucked ears of wheat, and rubbed them in their hands and did eat.
6.2: But some of the men of the Pharisees said to them, Why are you doing what is unlawful to do on the sabbath?
6.3: Jesus answered and said to them, Have you not read this, what David did when he and those who were with him were hungry?
6.4: He entered into the house of God, and took the bread that was on the table of the Lord and did eat it, and he gave it to those who were with him; that which was unlawful to eat but only for the priests.
6.5: And he said to them, The Son of man is Lord of the sabbath.
6.6: And it came to pass on another sabbath, he entered into the synagogue and taught; and there was there a man whose right hand was withered.
6.7: And the scribes and the Pharisees watched him, to see if he would heal on the sabbath, so that they might find an accusation against him.
6.8: But he knew their thoughts, and said to the man whose hand was withered, Rise up and come to the center of the synagogue. And when he came and stood up,
6.9: Jesus said to them, I will ask you, What is lawful to do on the sabbath, that which is good or that which is bad? to save a life or to destroy it?
6.10: And he looked at all of them, and said to him, Stretch out your hand. And he stretched it out; and his hand was restored like the other.
6.11: But they were filled with bitterness, and discussed with each other what to do with Jesus.
6.12: It happened in those days, Jesus went out to a mountain to pray, and he remained all night in prayer to God.
6.13: And at daybreak, he called his disciples; and he chose twelve from them, whom he called apostles;
6.14: Simon who is called Peter, and Andrew his brother, and James and John, and Philip and Bartholomew,
6.15: And Matthew and Thomas, and James the son of Alphaeus and Simon who is called the Zealot,
6.16: And Judas the son of James, and Judas of Iscariot, who became the traitor.
6.17: And Jesus went down with them and stood up in the plain; and a large group of his disciples, and a large crowd of people, from all over Judaea, and from Jerusalem, and from the sea coast of Tyre and Sidon, who had come to hear his word, and to be healed of their diseases;
6.18: And those who were suffering from unclean spirits were healed.
6.19: And all the people wanted to touch him; because power proceeded from him, and he healed them all.
6.20: And he lifted up his eyes on his disciples and said, Blessed are you poor, for the kingdom of God is yours.
6.21: Blessed are you who hunger now, for you shall be filled. Blessed are you who weep now, for you shall laugh.
6.22: Blessed are you, when men hate you, and discriminate against you, and reproach you, and publish your names as bad, for the sake of the Son of man.
6.23: Be glad and rejoice in that day, for your reward is increased in heaven; for their fathers did the same to the prophets.
6.24: But woe to you, rich men! for you have already received your comforts.
6.25: Woe to you who are full! for you will hunger. Woe to you who laugh now! for you will weep and mourn.
6.26: Woe to you when men speak well of you! for so did their fathers to the false prophets.
6.27: But I say to you who hear, Love your enemies, and do good to those who hate you,
6.28: And bless those who curse you, and pray for those who compel you to carry burdens.
6.29: And he who strikes you on your cheek, offer him the other; and he who takes away your robe, do not refuse your shirt also.
6.30: Give to every one who asks you; and from him who takes away what is yours, do not demand it back again.
6.31: Just as you want men to do to you. do to them likewise.
6.32: For if you love those who love you, what is your favor? for even sinners love those who love them.
6.33: And if you do good only to those who do good to you, what is your favor? for sinners also do the same.
6.34: And if you lend only to him from whom you expect to be paid back, what is your favor? for sinners also lend to sinners, to be paid back likewise.
6.35: But love your enemies, and do good to them, and lend, and do not cut off any man’s hope; so your reward will increase, and you will become sons of the Highest; for he is gracious to the wicked and the cruel.
6.36: Be therefore merciful, as your Father also is merciful.
6.37: Judge not, and you will not be judged; condemn not, and you will not be condemned; forgive, and you will be forgiven.
6.38: Give, and it will be given to you; good measure shaken up and running over, they will pour into your robe. For with the measure that you measure, it will be measured to you.
6.39: And he told them a parable, Can a blind man take care of a blind man? will they not both fall in a pit?
6.40: There is no disciple who is more important than his teacher; for every man who is well developed will be like his teacher.
6.41: Why do you see the splinter in your brother’s eye, and do not see the beam in your own eye?
6.42: Or how can you say to your brother, My brother, let me take out the splinter from your eye, when behold, you do not see the beam in your own eye? O hypocrites, first take out the beam from your own eye, and then you will see clearly to take out the splinter from your brother’s eye.
6.43: There is no good tree that bears bad fruits, nor a bad tree that bears good fruits.
6.44: For every tree is known by its own fruits. For they do not gather figs from thistles, nor gather grapes from a bramble bush.
6.45: A good man brings out good things from the good treasure of his heart; and a bad man from the bad treasure of his heart brings out bad things; for from the abundance of the heart the lips speak.
6.46: Why do you call me, My Lord, my Lord, and do not do what I say?
6.47: Every man who comes to me and hears my words and does them, I will show you what he is like.
6.48: He is like a man who built a house, and dug deep, and laid its foundations upon the rock; and when the flood came, the flood beat upon that house, and could not shake it; for its foundation was laid upon a rock.
6.49: And he who hears and does not, is like a man who built his house on the earth without a foundation; and when the river beat against it, it fell immediately, and the fall of that house was great.

7.1: WHEN he had finished all of these words, in the hearing of the people, Jesus entered Capernaum.
7.2: Now the servant of a centurion was seriously sick, one who was very dear to him; and he was near death.
7.3: And when he heard about Jesus, he sent to him Jewish elders, and besought him to come and heal his servant.
7.4: When they came to Jesus, they begged him earnestly, saying, He is worthy to have this done for him;
7.5: For he loves our people, and has even built us a synagogue.
7.6: Jesus went with them. And when he was not far from the house, the centurion sent some of his friends to him, and said, My Lord, do not trouble yourself; for I am not worthy that you should enter under my roof;
7.7: That is why I was not worthy to come to you; but just say a word and my boy will be healed.
7.8: For I am also a man in government service, and there are soldiers under my command; and I say to this one, Go, and he goes; and to another, Come, and he comes; and to my servant, Do this, and he does it.
7.9: When Jesus heard these things, he was amazed at him, and he turned and said to the people who followed him, I say to you, not even in Israel have I found such faith as this.
7.10: So those who were sent returned to the house, and found the servant who was sick, healed.
7.11: And it came to pass on the next day, he was going to a city called Nain; and his disciples were with him, and many people.
7.12: And when they came near the gate of the city, he saw a dead man being carried out, who was the only son of his mother, and his mother was a widow; and many people of the city were with her.
7.13: When Jesus saw her, he had compassion on her, and said to her, Weep not.
7.14: Then he went and touched the bier, and those who carried it stood still. And he said, Young man, I tell you, Arise.
7.15: And the dead man sat up, and began to speak. And he gave him to his mother.
7.16: And all men were seized with fear; and they praised God, saying, A great prophet is risen among us; and, God has visited his people.
7.17: And this word about him went out through all Judaea, and through the country around them.
7.18: And John’s disciples told him all these things.
7.19: So John called two of his disciples, and sent them to Jesus, and said, Are you the one who is to come? or are we to expect another one?
7.20: And they came to Jesus and said to him, John the Baptist has sent us to you, saying, Are you the one who is to come? or are we to expect another one?
7.21: In that very hour, he healed a great many of their diseases and plagues, and of evil spirits; and he gave sight to many blind men.
7.22: So Jesus answered and said to them, Go and tell John everything that you have seen and heard; that the blind see, and the lame walk, and the lepers are cleansed, and the deaf hear, and the dead rise up, and the poor are given hope.
7.23: And blessed is he, who does not stumble on account of me.
7.24: When John’s disciples had gone, Jesus began to speak to the people concerning John, What did you go out to the wilderness to see? A reed which is shaken by the wind?
7.25: If not so, what did you go out to see? A man dressed in fine clothes? Behold, those who wear fine clothes and live delicately are in kings’ houses.
7.26: And if not so, what did you go out to see? A prophet? Yes, I say to you, and much more than a prophet.
7.27: This is he of whom it is written, Behold, I send my messenger before your face, to prepare the way before you.
7.28: I say to you that there is no prophet among those who are born of women, who is greater than John the Baptist; and yet even the least person in the kingdom of God is greater than he.
7.29: And all the people who heard it, even the publicans, justified themselves before God, for they were baptized with the baptism of John.
7.30: But the Pharisees and the scribes suppressed the will of God in themselves, because they were not baptized by him.
7.31: To whom, therefore, shall I liken the men of this generation? and to what are they like?
7.32: They are like boys who sit in the street, and call to their friends and say, We have sung to you but you did not dance; and we have wailed to you and you did not weep.
7.33: For John the Baptist came, neither eating bread nor drinking wine; and you say, He is insane.
7.34: The Son of man came, eating and drinking; and you say, Behold, a glutton and a winebibber, and a friend of publicans and sinners!
7.35: And yet wisdom is justified by all its works.
7.36: Then one of the Pharisees came and asked him to eat with him. And he entered the house of that Pharisee and reclined as a guest.
7.37: Now there was in that city a woman who was a sinner; and when she knew that he was a guest in the Pharisee’s house, she took an alabaster cruse of perfume,
7.38: And she stood behind him at his feet, weeping, and she began to wet his feet with her tears, and to wipe them with the hair of her head, and she kissed his feet, and anointed them with perfume.
7.39: When the Pharisee who had invited him saw it, he reasoned in himself and said, If this man were a prophet, he would have known who she was and her reputation; for the woman who has touched him is a sinner.
7.40: Jesus answered and said to him, Simon, I have something to tell you. He said to him, Say it, teacher. Jesus said to him,
7.41: There were two men who were debtors to a creditor; one of them owed him five hundred pence, and the other one fifty pence.
7.42: And because they had nothing to pay, he forgave them both. Which one of them will love him more?
7.43: Simon answered and said, I think the one to whom he forgave more. Jesus said to him, You have judged truly.
7.44: And he turned to the woman, and said to Simon, Do you see this woman? I entered your house, you did not give me even water for my feet; but she has wet my feet with her tears, and wiped them with her hair.
7.45: You did not kiss me; but she, since she entered, has not ceased to kiss my feet.
7.46: You did not anoint my head with oil; but she has anointed my feet with perfume.
7.47: For this reason, I say to you, Her many sins are forgiven, because she loved much; but he to whom little is forgiven, loves little.
7.48: And he said to the woman, Your sins are forgiven.
7.49: Then the guests began to say within themselves, Who is this man, who forgives even sins?
7.50: Jesus said to the woman, Your faith has saved you; go in peace.

8.1: AND it came to pass after these things, Jesus was traveling in cities and villages, preaching and giving good news of the kingdom of God. And his twelve were with him,
8.2: And the women who were healed of diseases and unclean spirits, Mary who is called of Magdala, from whom seven demons went out,
8.3: And Joanna, the wife of Chuza the steward of Herod, and Susanna, and many others, who ministered to them of their wealth.
8.4: And when many people had gathered, and were coming to him from all the cities, he spoke by parables.
8.5: The sower went out to sow his seed. And when he sowed, some fell on the roadside; and it was trodden under foot, and the birds ate it.
8.6: Other fell upon the rock; and sprung up earlier, and because it had no moisture, it dried up.
8.7: And other fell among thistles; and the thistles sprung up with it and choked it.
8.8: And other fell in good and fertile ground; and sprung up and bore fruit a hundredfold. And when he said this, he cried out, He who has ears to hear, let him hear.
8.9: And his disciples asked him, What is this parable?
8.10: He said to them, To you it is granted to know the mystery of the kingdom of God; but to the rest it has to be said in figures; for while they see, they do not perceive; and while they hear, they do not understand.
8.11: This is the parable. The seed is the word of God.
8.12: Those on the roadside are those who hear the word; and the enemy comes and takes away the word from their heart, so that they may not believe and be saved.
8.13: Those on the rock are those who when they have heard, receive the word with joy; and yet they have no root, but their belief is for a while, and in time of trial they stumble.
8.14: That which fell among the thistles are those who hear the word, and then choke themselves with worries and riches and worldly covetousness, and bear no fruit.
8.15: But that in good soil, these are those who hear the word with pure and good heart, and keep it, and bear fruit with patience.
8.16: No man lights a lamp and covers it with a vessel, or puts it under the bed; but he puts it on the lamp holder, that whoever enters sees its light.
8.17: For there is nothing covered which will not be uncovered; and nothing hidden which will not be known, and come to light.
8.18: Take heed how you hear; for he who has, to him shall be given; and he who has not, even that which he thinks he has shall be taken away from him.
8.19: And there came to him his mother and his brothers, and they were not able to speak to him because of the crowd.
8.20: And they said to him, Your mother and your brothers are standing outside, and they want to see you.
8.21: He answered and said to them, These are my mother and my brothers, those who hear the word of God and do it.
8.22: It came to pass on one of the days, Jesus went up and sat in a boat with his disciples; and he said to them, Let us cross to the other side of the lake.
8.23: And while they were rowing Jesus fell asleep; and there rose a storm of wind on the lake; and the boat was near sinking.
8.24: And they came up and awoke him and said to him, Our teacher, our teacher, we are perishing. He got up and rebuked the winds and the waves of water, and they quieted down, and there was a calm.
8.25: And he said to them, Where is your faith? But as they were frightened, they wondered, saying one to another, O who is this man, who even commands the winds, and the waves and the sea obey him?
8.26: And they rowed and came to the country of the Gadarenes, which is on the coast opposite Galilee.
8.27: And when he landed, he was met by a man from the city, who had the demon in him for a long time, and he did not wear clothes, and did not live in a house, but in the cemetery.
8.28: When he saw Jesus, he cried out and fell before him, and said in a loud voice, What have we in common, Jesus, Son of the Most High God? I beg you not to torment me.
8.29: For Jesus commanded the unclean spirit to go out of the man. For it was a long time since he was possessed, and bound with chains, and kept in fetters; but he would often break off his bonds and was driven into the desert by the demon.
8.30: Jesus asked him, What is your name? He said, Legion, because many demons had entered into him.
8.31: And they besought him not to command them to go down into the abyss.
8.32: Now there was there a herd of many swine feeding on the mountain; and they besought him to permit them to attack the swine. And he permitted them.
8.33: Then the demons went out of the man, and they attacked the swine; and that whole herd went straight to the cliff, and fell down into the lake and were drowned.
8.34: When the herdsmen saw what had happened, they fled and told it in the cities and in the villages.
8.35: And some men went out to see what had happened; and they came to Jesus, and found the man from whom the demons had gone out, dressed, and well behaved, and sitting at the feet of Jesus; and they were afraid.
8.36: And those who had seen it told them, how that lunatic was healed.
8.37: Then all the people of the Gadarenes besought him to leave them, because they were seized with a great fear; and Jesus went up into the boat and returned from thence.
8.38: But the man from whom the demons had gone out, besought him to remain with him; but Jesus dismissed him and said to him,
8.39: Return to your own house, and declare what God has done for you. And he went away, and preached throughout the city what Jesus had done for him.
8.40: When Jesus returned, a large multitude welcomed him, for they were all expecting him.
8.41: And a man named Jairus, a leader of the synagogue, fell at the feet of Jesus, and besought him to enter into his house.
8.42: For he had an only daughter, about twelve years old, and she was near death. And as Jesus went with him, a large crowd pressed against him.
8.43: Now a woman who had the hemorrhage for twelve years, and had spent all her wealth for doctors, could not be healed by anybody.
8.44: She came near him from behind, and touched the edge of his cloak; and immediately her hemorrhage stopped.
8.45: And Jesus said, Who touched me? And when all of them denied it, Simon Peter and those who were with him said to him, Teacher, the crowds are troubling you and pressing on you, and yet you say, Who has touched me?
8.46: But he said, Some one has touched me, for I know that power has gone out of me.
8.47: When the woman saw that she could not deceive him, she came trembling, and fell down and worshipped him; and she said in the presence of all the people for what purpose she had touched him, and how she was healed immediately.
8.48: Jesus said to her, Have courage, my daughter; your faith has healed you; go in peace.
8.49: While he was still talking, there came a man from the house of the leader of the synagogue, and said to him, Your daughter has died, do not trouble the teacher.
8.50: Jesus heard it and said to the father of the girl, Do not be afraid, but only believe, and she will be restored to life.
8.51: Jesus came into the house, and he did not allow anyone to enter with him, except Simon and James and John, and the father and mother of the girl.
8.52: And all of them were weeping and mourning over her; but Jesus said, Do not weep, for she is not dead but asleep.
8.53: And they laughed at him, for they knew that she was dead.
8.54: Then he put everybody out, and held her by her hand, and called her, and said, Little girl, arise.
8.55: And her spirit returned, and she got up immediately; and he commanded to give her something to eat.
8.56: And her parents were amazed; but he warned them, not to tell any man what had happened.

9.1: THEN Jesus called his twelve, and gave them power and authority over all the demons, and to cure diseases.
9.2: And he sent them out to preach the kingdom of God, and to heal the sick.
9.3: And he said to them, Do not take anything for the journey, neither a staff, nor a bag, nor bread, nor money; nor have two shirts.
9.4: And into whatever house you enter, remain there, and depart from thence.
9.5: And whoever will not welcome you, when you leave that city, shake off even the sand from your feet for a testimony to them.
9.6: And the apostles went out, and travelled in villages and cities, preaching the gospel, and healing everywhere.
9.7: Now Herod the tetrarch heard of all that was done by his hand; and he was amazed, because some men said that John has risen from the dead.
9.8: But others, that Elijah has appeared; and others, that one of the old prophets has risen.
9.9: So Herod said, I have beheaded John; but who is this one concerning whom I hear these things? And he wanted to see him.
9.10: When the apostles returned, they told Jesus everything which they had done. And he took them all alone to a lonely place in Bethsaida.
9.11: When the people found it out, they went after him; and he received them, and spoke to them concerning the kingdom of God, and he healed those who were in need of healing.
9.12: And when the day began to wane, his disciples came up and said to him, Dismiss the people, that they may go to the villages around us and to the farms, to lodge there, and find food for themselves; because we are in a lonely place.
9.13: Jesus said to them, You give them to eat. But they said, We do not have more than five loaves of bread and two fish; unless we go and buy food for all this people;
9.14: For there were about five thousand men. Jesus said to them, Make them sit down in groups, fifty men in each group.
9.15: The disciples did so, and made them all sit down.
9.16: And Jesus took the five loaves of bread and the two fish, and looked up to heaven, and he blessed them, and broke and gave them to his disciples, to set before the people.
9.17: And they all ate and were filled; and they took up fragments of what was left over, twelve baskets.
9.18: While he prayed by himself, and his disciples were with him, he asked them and said, What do the people say concerning me that I am?
9.19: They answered and said to him, John the Baptist; and others, Elijah; and others that one of the old prophets has risen.
9.20: He said to them, But you, what do you say that I am? Simon answered and said, The Messiah (the anointed one of God).
9.21: But he cautioned them, and warned them not to say this to anyone.
9.22: And he said to them, The Son of man must suffer a great many things, and he will be rejected by the elders and the high priests and the scribes, and they will kill him, and on the third day he will rise.
9.23: Then he said in the presence of everyone, He who wishes to come after me, let him deny himself, and take up his cross every day and follow me.
9.24: For he who wishes to save his life, shall lose it; but he who loses his life for my sake, he shall save it.
9.25: For how can a man be benefited, if he gain the whole world, but lose his own soul, or even weakens it?
9.26: For whoever is ashamed of me and of my words, the Son of man will be ashamed of him, when he comes with the glory of his Father, accompanied by his holy angels.
9.27: I tell you the truth, that there are men who stand here, who will not taste death, until they see the kingdom of God.
9.28: And it came to pass about eight days after these words, Jesus took Simon, and James and John, and went up into a mountain to pray.
9.29: And while he prayed, the appearance of his face was changed, and his clothes became white and dazzling.
9.30: And behold, two men were speaking with him, who were Moses and Elijah;
9.31: Who appeared in glory, and spoke concerning his departure which was to end at Jerusalem.
9.32: And Simon and those who were with him were heavy with sleep; and when they awoke they saw his glory, and the two men who stood with him.
9.33: And when they began to leave him, Simon said to Jesus, Teacher, it is better for us to remain here; and let us make three shelters, one for you, one for Moses, and one for Elijah; but he did not know what he was saying.
9.34: And when he had said these things, there came a cloud and overshadowed them; and they were frightened when they saw Moses and Elijah enter into the cloud.
9.35: And there came a voice out of the cloud, saying, This is my beloved Son; hear him.
9.36: And when the voice was heard, they found Jesus alone. And they kept silent, and in those days they did not tell any man what they saw.
9.37: And it came to pass the next day, as they came down from the mountain, they were met by many people.
9.38: And one of the men of that crowd cried out and said, O teacher, I beg you to have mercy on me. I have an only son,
9.39: And a spirit seizes him, and he suddenly cries out, and gnashes his teeth and foams; and it hardly leaves him when it has tormented him.
9.40: And I besought your disciples to cast it out; and they could not.
9.41: Jesus answered and said, O crooked and faithless generation, how long will I be with you, and preach to you? Bring your son here.
9.42: And as he brought him, the demon attacked him and convulsed him. And Jesus rebuked the unclean spirit, and healed the boy, and gave him to his father.
9.43: And they were all amazed at the greatness of God. And while every man wondered at everything which Jesus did, he said to his disciples,
9.44: Treasure these words in your ears; for the Son of man will be delivered into the hands of men.
9.45: But they did not understand this word, because it was hidden from them so that they might not know it; and they were afraid to ask him concerning this word.
9.46: Then a reasoning entered into their minds, as to who was the greatest among them.
9.47: But Jesus knew the reasoning of their heart, and he took a boy and made him stand by him.
9.48: And he said to them, Everyone who receives a little boy like this one in my name, receives me; and he who receives me receives him who sent me; for whoever is least among you, let him be great.
9.49: And John answered and said, Teacher, we saw a man casting out demons in your name; and we forbad him, because he did not come with us, as your follower.
9.50: Jesus said to them, Do not forbid; for he who is not against you is for you.
9.51: And it happened, when the days to go up on his journey were fulfilled, he set his face to go to Jerusalem.
9.52: So he sent messengers ahead of him; and they went away and entered into a Samaritan village, to prepare for him.
9.53: But they did not receive him, because his face was set to go straight to Jerusalem.
9.54: When his disciples James and John saw it, they said to him, Our Lord, would you be willing that we command fire to come down from heaven and consume them, just as Elijah did?
9.55: He turned and rebuked them and said, You do not know of what spirit you are.
9.56: For the Son of man did not come to destroy lives, but to save. And they went to another village.
9.57: And while they were on the journey, a man said to him, My Lord, I will follow you wherever you go.
9.58: Jesus said to him, The foxes have holes, and the fowl of the sky a shelter; but the Son of man has no place even to lay his head.
9.59: He said to another, Follow me; but he said to him, My Lord, permit me first to go and bury my father.
9.60: Jesus said to him, Let the dead bury their own dead; but you go and preach the kingdom of God.
9.61: Another one said to him, I will follow you, my Lord; but permit me first to entrust my household to some one, and then come.
9.62: Jesus said to him, No man who puts his hand on the plough handle, and looks back, is fit for the kingdom of God.

10.1: AFTER these things, Jesus selected from his disciples seventy others, and he sent them two by two before his face, to every place and city to which he was to go.
10.2: And he said to them, The harvest is great, and the laborers are few; ask therefore the owner of the harvest, to bring out laborers to his harvest.
10.3: Go forth; behold, I send you as lambs among wolves.
10.4: Do not carry purses, nor bags, nor shoes; and do not salute any man on the road.
10.5: And to whatever house you enter, first say, Peace be to this house.
10.6: And if a man of peace is there, let your peace rest upon him; and if not, your peace will return to you.
10.7: Remain in that house, eating and drinking of what they have; for a laborer is worthy of his wages. Do not keep moving from house to house.
10.8: And into whatever city you enter, and they receive you, eat whatever they set before you;
10.9: And heal those who are sick in it, and say to them, The kingdom of God is come near to you.
10.10: But into whatever city you enter, and they do not receive you, go out into the street and say,
10.11: Even the sand of your city which cleaves to our feet, we shake it off to you; but know this that the kingdom of God has come near to you.
10.12: I say to you, that it will be much easier for Sodom in that day than for that city.
10.13: Woe to you, Chorazin! Woe to you, Bethsaida! If the mighty works which were done in you, had been done in Tyre and Sidon, perhaps they might have repented with sackcloth and ashes.
10.14: But, it will be easier for Tyre and Sidon at the judgment day than for you.
10.15: And you, Capernaum, which have exalted yourself up to heaven, you will be brought down to Sheol.
10.16: He who hears you hears me; and he who oppresses you oppresses me; and he who oppresses me oppresses him who sent me.
10.17: So the seventy whom he had sent returned with great joy, and they said to him, Our Lord, even the demons have submitted to us in your name.
10.18: He said to them, I saw Satan falling like lightning from heaven.
10.19: Behold, I give you power, to tread on snakes and scorpions, and over all the power of the enemy; and nothing shall harm you.
10.20: But do not rejoice in this that the demons submit to you; but rejoice because your names are written in heaven.
10.21: At that very hour, Jesus rejoiced in the Holy Spirit and said, I thank you, O my Father, Lord of heaven and earth, because you did hide these things from the wise and men of understanding, and did reveal them to children; yes, my Father, for so it was well pleasing in your presence.
10.22: And he turned to his disciples and said to them, Everything has been entrusted to me by my Father; and no man knows who is the Son, except the Father; and who is the Father except the Son, and to whomever the Son wishes to reveal him.
10.23: Then he turned to his disciples alone and said, Blessed are the eyes which see what you see.
10.24: For I say to you, that many prophets and kings desired to see what you see, and did not see it; and to hear what you hear, and did not hear it.
10.25: And behold, a scribe stood up to test him, and he said, Teacher, what shall I do to inherit eternal life?
10.26: Jesus said to him, What is written in the law? how do you read it?
10.27: He answered and said to him, You must love the Lord your God with all your heart, and with all your soul, and with all your strength, and with all your mind; and your neighbor as yourself.
10.28: Jesus said to him, You said the truth; do this and you shall live.
10.29: But as he wanted to justify himself, he said to him, And who is my neighbor?
10.30: Jesus said to him, There was a man who went down from Jerusalem to Jericho, and the bandits attacked him, and robbed him, and beat him, and left him with little life remaining in him, and they went away.
10.31: And it chanced a priest was going down that road; and he saw him and passed on.
10.32: And likewise a Levite came and arrived at that place, and saw him and passed on.
10.33: But a Samaritan, as he journeyed, came where he was, and when he saw him, he had compassion on him.
10.34: And he came to him and bound up his wounds, and poured on them wine and oil; and he put him on his own ass, and brought him to the inn, and took care of him.
10.35: And in the morning, he took out two pennies and gave them to the innkeeper, and said to him, Take care of him; and whatever you spend more, when I return, I will give it to you.
10.36: Who therefore of these three, appears to you, became neighbor to him who fell into the hands of the bandits?
10.37: He said, The one who had compassion on him. Jesus said to him, You go also, and do the same.
10.38: And it came to pass while they were journeying, he entered into a village; and a woman named Martha received him into her house.
10.39: And she had a sister whose name was Mary, and she came and sat at the feet of our Lord, and listened to his words.
10.40: But Martha was busy with many household cares, and she came and said to him, My Lord, you do not seem to care that my sister has left me to serve alone? tell her to help me.
10.41: Jesus answered and said to her, Martha, Martha, you are worried and excited about many things;
10.42: But one thing is more necessary; and Mary has chosen the good portion for herself, which shall not be taken away from her.

11.1: AND it came to pass, while he was praying in a certain place, when he finished, one of his disciples said to him, Our Lord, teach us to pray, just as John also taught his disciples.
11.2: Jesus said to them, When you pray, say like this, Our Father in heaven, Hallowed be thy name. Thy kingdom come. Let thy will be done, as in heaven, so on earth.
11.3: Give us bread for our needs every day.
11.4: And forgive us our sins; for we have also forgiven all who are indebted to us. And do not let us enter into temptation; but deliver us from error.
11.5: And he said to them, Who is among you who has a friend, and he should go to him at midnight, and say to him, My friend, loan me three loaves,
11.6: For a friend has come to me from a journey, and I have nothing to set before him.
11.7: Would his friend from inside answer and say to him, Do not trouble me; the door is already locked, and my children are with me in bed; I cannot get up and give you.
11.8: I say to you, that if because of friendship he will not give him, yet because of his persistence, he will rise and give him as much as he wants.
11.9: I say to you also, Ask, and it shall be given to you; seek, and you shall find; knock, and it shall be opened to you.
11.10: For everyone who asks, receives; and he who seeks, finds; and he who knocks, it is opened to him.
11.11: For who is among you, a father, if his son should ask him bread, why, would he hand him a stone? and if he should ask him a fish, why, would he hand him a snake instead of a fish?
11.12: And if he should ask him for an egg, why, would he hand him a scorpion?
11.13: So if you, who err, know how to give good gifts to your children, how much more will your Father give the Holy Spirit from heaven to those who ask him?
11.14: And while he was casting out a demon from a dumb man, it came to pass when the demon went out, the dumb man spoke; and the people were amazed.
11.15: But some of the men among them said, This man casts out devils by Beelzebub, the prince of devils.
11.16: And others, tempting him, asked him for a sign from heaven.
11.17: But Jesus knew their thoughts, and said to them, Every kingdom which is divided against itself, shall be destroyed; and a house which is divided against itself shall fall.
11.18: And if Satan is divided against himself, how can his kingdom survive? And yet you say I am casting out devils through Beelzebub.
11.19: If I cast out devils through Beelzebub, by what do your sons cast them out? Therefore they will be your judges.
11.20: But if I cast out devils by the finger of God, then the kingdom of God is come near you.
11.21: When a strong man is armed and keeps watch over his courtyard, his property is safe;
11.22: But if there should come one who is stronger than he, he will conquer him, and take away his armor in which he trusted, and divide his spoil.
11.23: He who is not with me is against me; and he who does not gather with me will scatter.
11.24: When an unclean spirit is gone out of a man, it goes away and travels in places where there is no water, to seek rest; and when it finds it not, it says, I will return to my own house from whence I came out.
11.25: And if it should come and find it warm and well furnished,
11.26: Then it goes away and brings seven other spirits worse than itself; and they enter and dwell there; and the end of that man will become worse than the beginning.
11.27: While he was saying these things, a woman out of the multitude lifted up her voice and said to him, Blessed is the womb which bore you, and the breasts which gave you suck.
11.28: He said to her, Blessed are they who hear the word of God and keep it.
11.29: And when the people were gathering, he began to say, This evil generation wants a sign; and no sign will be given to it, except the sign of the prophet Jonah.
11.30: For as Jonah was a sign to the Ninevites, so also will the Son of man be to this generation.
11.31: The queen of the south will rise up in judgment with the men of this generation, and condemn them; for she came from the far ends of the earth to hear the wisdom of Solomon; and behold, a greater than Solomon is here.
11.32: The men of Nineveh will rise up in judgment against this generation and condemn it; for they repented at the preaching of Jonah; and behold, a greater than Jonah is here.
11.33: No man lights a lamp and puts it in a hidden place, or under a basket, but on a lamp holder, so that those who enter may see its light.
11.34: The lamp of your body is your eye; when therefore your eye is clear, your whole body will also be lighted; but if it is diseased, your whole body will also be dark.
11.35: Take heed, therefore, lest the light which is in you be darkness.
11.36: If your whole body is lighted, and there is no part in it dark, the whole of it will give light, just as a lamp gives you light with its shining.
11.37: While he spoke, a Pharisee asked him to dine with him; and he entered and reclined.
11.38: When the Pharisee saw him, he was amazed because he did not first wash before dinner.
11.39: And Jesus said to him, Now you Pharisees clean the outside of the cup and the dish; but within you are full of extortion and iniquity.
11.40: O you shortsighted, did not he who made the outside also make the inside?
11.41: But give alms of what you have; and, behold, everything will be clean to you.
11.42: But woe to you Pharisees! who take tithes of mint and dill and every kind of vegetable, but overlook justice and the love of God. These were necessary for you to have done, and the same by no means to have left undone.
11.43: Woe to you Pharisees! for you love chief seats in the synagogues, and salutations in the streets.
11.44: Woe to you, scribes and Pharisees, hypocrites! for you are like graves that cannot be recognized, and men walk over them and know it not.
11.45: One of the scribes answered and said to him, Teacher, when you say these things, you reproach us also.
11.46: But he said, Woe also to you, scribes! for you lay heavy burdens on men, and you yourselves do not touch these burdens even with one of your fingers.
11.47: Woe to you! for you build the tombs of prophets, whom your fathers killed.
11.48: Therefore you are witnesses, and you approve the works of your fathers; for they killed them, and yet you build their tombs.
11.49: For this reason, the wisdom of God also said, Behold, I will send them prophets and apostles, some of them they will persecute and kill;
11.50: That the blood of all the prophets, which was shed since the creation of the world, may be avenged on this generation;
11.51: From the blood of Abel to the blood of Zacharias, who was killed between the temple and the altar; yes, I say to you, it will be avenged on this generation.
11.52: Woe to you, scribes! for you have taken away the keys of knowledge; you did not enter, and those who were entering you hindered.
11.53: When he had said these things to them, the scribes and the Pharisees were displeased, and they were enraged and criticized his words.
11.54: And they plotted against him in many ways, seeking to catch something from his mouth, so that they might be able to accuse him.

12.1: WHEN a large number of people had gathered together, so as to tread on one another, Jesus began to say to his disciples, First of all, Beware you of the leaven of the Pharisees, which is hypocrisy.
12.2: For there is nothing that is covered, that will not be uncovered; and what is hidden that will not be known.
12.3: For whatever you have said in darkness will be heard in the light; and what you have whispered in the ears in the inner chambers will be preached on the housetops.
12.4: I say to you, my friends, Do not be afraid of those who kill the body, and after that have nothing more they can do.
12.5: But I will show you of whom to be afraid; of him who after he has killed has the power to throw into hell; yes, I say to you, Fear him.
12.6: Are not five sparrows sold for two pennies? And yet not one of them is lost before God.
12.7: But so far as you are concerned, even the hairs of your head are all numbered; therefore fear not, because you are much more important than many sparrows.
12.8: I say to you, Whoever will acknowledge me before men, the Son of man will also acknowledge him before the angels of God.
12.9: But he who denies me before men, I will deny him before the angels of God.
12.10: And whoever says a word against the Son of man, will be forgiven; but he who blasphemes against the Holy Spirit will not be forgiven.
12.11: When they bring you to the synagogues before the leaders and authorities, do not worry how you will answer, or what you will say;
12.12: For the Holy Spirit will teach you at that very hour what you ought to say.
12.13: And one of the men from the crowd said to him, Teacher, speak to my brother to divide the inheritance with me.
12.14: Jesus said to him, Man, who appointed me a judge or a property divider over you?
12.15: And he said to his disciples, Beware of all covetousness, because life does not depend on abundance of wealth.
12.16: Then he told them a parable. The land of a rich man brought him a great many crops.
12.17: And he reasoned within himself and said, What shall I do, for I have no place to gather my crops?
12.18: So he said, I will do this; I will tear down my barns, and build them and enlarge them; and gather there all my wheat and my good things.
12.19: And I will say to myself, Myself, you have many good things stored up for many years; rest, eat, drink, and be happy.
12.20: But God said to him, O you shortsighted, this very night your life will be demanded of you; and these things which you have prepared, to whom will they he left?
12.21: Such is he who lays up treasures for himself, and is not rich in the things in God.
12.22: And he said to his disciples, Therefore I say to you, Do not worry for your life, what you will eat; nor for your body, what you will wear.
12.23: For the life is much more important than food, and the body than clothing.
12.24: Observe the ravens; for they do not sow nor reap, and they have no storerooms and barns; and yet God feeds them; how much more important are you than the fowls?
12.25: Who is among you, who by worrying, can add to his stature one cubit?
12.26: So if you are not able to do the smaller thing, why do you worry about the rest?
12.27: Observe the flowers, how they grow; for they do not toil nor do they spin; but I say to you, that not even Solomon with all his glory was covered like one of these.
12.28: And if God clothes in such fashion the grass of the field, which today is and tomorrow falls into the fireplace; how much more is he to you, O you of little faith?
12.29: So do not be anxious what you will eat, and what you will drink, and let not your mind be disturbed by these things.
12.30: For worldly people seek after all these things; and your Father knows that these things are also necessary for you.
12.31: But you, seek the kingdom of God; and all of these things shall be added to you.
12.32: Do not be afraid, O little flock; for your Father is pleased to give you the kingdom.
12.33: Sell your possessions and give them as alms; make for yourselves purses which do not wear out, and a treasure in heaven that does not run short, where the thief does not come near, and moth does not destroy.
12.34: For where your treasure is, there also will be your heart.
12.35: Let your girdle be fastened on your loins, and your lamps lighted.
12.36: And be like men who expect their master, when he will return from the wedding house; so that when he comes and knocks, they will immediately open the door for him.
12.37: Blessed are those servants, whom their master, when he comes, finds awake; truly I say to you, that he will gird himself and make them sit down, and come in, and serve them.
12.38: If he should come in the second or the third watch and find them so, blessed are those servants.
12.39: But know this, that if the master of the house knew at what watch the thief would come, he would have kept awake, and not allowed his house to be plundered.
12.40: Therefore, you also be ready; for the Son of man will come in that very hour which you do not expect.
12.41: Simon Peter said to him, Our Lord, do you speak this parable to us, or also to all men?
12.42: Jesus said to him, Who is the faithful and wise steward, whom his master will appoint over his household, to give supplies in due time?
12.43: Blessed is that servant, whom when his master comes will find him so doing.
12.44: Truly I say to you, that he will appoint him over all his wealth.
12.45: But if that servant should say in his heart, My master has delayed his coming; and begins to beat the servants and maidservants of his master, and then begins to eat and drink and get drunk;
12.46: The master of that servant will come in a day and at an hour that he does not expect or know; and he will severely punish him, and place him with those who are not trustworthy.
12.47: And the servant who knows the wishes of his master, and does not make ready according to his wishes, will receive a severe beating.
12.48: But he who does not know, and does what is worthy of punishment will receive less beating. For to whomever more is given, of him more will be required; and to whom much is entrusted, more will be required of his hand.
12.49: I came to set the earth on fire; and I wish to do it, if it has not already been kindled.
12.50: I have a baptism to be baptized with; and I am oppressed until it is fulfilled.
12.51: Do you think that I have come to bring peace on earth? I say to you, No, but divisions;
12.52: For from henceforth there will be five in a house, who will be divided, three against two, and two against three.
12.53: For a father will be divided against his son, and a son against his father; a mother against her daughter, and a daughter against her mother; a mother-in-law against her daughter-in-law, and a daughter-in-law against her mother-in-law.
12.54: And he said to the people, When you see a cloud rise from the west, you immediately say, It will rain; and it is so.
12.55: And when the wind blows from the south, you say, It will be hot; and it is so.
12.56: O you hypocrites, you know how to discern the face of the earth and of the sky; how then is it that you do not discern this time?
12.57: Why do you not of yourselves judge what is right?
12.58: For when you go with your accuser to the district leader, while you are on the way give something and settle with him; otherwise he might take you to the judge, and the judge will deliver you to the prison warden, and the prison warden will throw you in prison.
12.59: Truly I say to you, you will not come out from thence, until you pay the last penny.

13.1: AT that time, there came some men and told him about the Galileans, whose blood Pilate had mingled with their sacrifices.
13.2: And Jesus answered and said to them, Do you think that those Galileans were greater sinners than all the Galileans, because this happened to them?
13.3: No; but I say to you, that all of you also, if you do not repent, you will perish in the same way.
13.4: Or those eighteen, upon whom the tower in Siloam fell, and it killed them; do you think that they were greater sinners than all the men who live in Jerusalem?
13.5: No, but I say to you, that unless you repent, all of you will perish like them.
13.6: And he spoke this parable; A man had a fig tree planted in his vineyard; and he came and sought fruit on it, and he did not find any.
13.7: So he said to the laborer, Behold, for three years, I have been coming and seeking fruit on this fig tree, and found none; cut it down; why should it waste the ground?
13.8: The laborer said to him, My lord, let it remain this year also, until I work it and fertilize it.
13.9: It might bear fruit; and if not, then you can cut it down.
13.10: While Jesus was teaching in one of the synagogues on the sabbath,
13.11: There was there a woman who was afflicted with rheumatism for eighteen years; and was bent down and could never straighten herself at all.
13.12: Jesus saw her, and called her, and said to her, Woman, you are loosened from your sickness.
13.13: And he laid his hand on her, and immediately she straightened up, and praised God.
13.14: But the leader of the synagogue answered with anger, because Jesus healed on the sabbath; and he said to the people, There are six days in which men should work; in those days you ought to come and be healed, and not on the sabbath day.
