13.15: Jesus answered and said to him, O hypocrites, does not each one of you loosen his ox or his ass, from the manger, and go with it to give it drink?
13.16: This one is a daughter of Abraham, and behold, the adversary has bound her for eighteen years; was it not necessary for her to be loosened from this bond on the sabbath day?
13.17: And when he said these things, all who opposed him were ashamed; and all the people rejoiced over all the wonders which were done by his hand.
13.18: Jesus said, To what is the kingdom of God like? and to what shall I liken it?
13.19: It is like a grain of mustard seed, which a man took and cast into his garden, and it grew and became a large tree, and the fowls of the sky settled on its branches.
13.20: Again Jesus said, To what shall I liken the kingdom of God?
13.21: It is like the leaven which a woman took and buried in three measures of flour, until it was all leavened.
13.22: And he journeyed through the villages and cities, teaching, and going to Jerusalem.
13.23: A man asked him, Are there only a few who are to be saved? Jesus said to them,
13.24: Strive to enter in through the narrow door; for I say to you, that many will seek to enter in, and will not be able.
13.25: From the hour when the master of the house rises up and locks the door, you will be standing outside and knocking at the door, and you will begin to say, Our Lord, our Lord, open for us; and he will answer and say, I say to you, I do not know you where you come from.
13.26: And you will begin to say, We have eaten and drunk in your presence, and you taught in our streets.
13.27: And he will say to you, I do not know you where you come from; depart from me, O you workers of iniquity.
13.28: There will be weeping and gnashing of teeth, when you see Abraham, and Isaac and Jacob, and all the prophets in the kingdom of God, but you thrown outside.
13.29: And they will come from the east and from the west, and from the south and from the north, and sit down in the kingdom of God.
13.30: And behold, there are some who are last, who will be first, and there are some who are first who will be last.
13.31: In that very day, some of the men of the Pharisees drew near and said to him, Get out and go away from here; because Herod wants to kill you.
13.32: Jesus said to them, Go and tell that fox, Behold, I cast out demons, and I heal today and tomorrow, and on the third day I will be through.
13.33: But I must do my work today and tomorrow, and I will leave the next day; because it is impossible that a prophet should perish outside of Jerusalem.
13.34: O Jerusalem, Jerusalem, murderess of prophets, and stoner of those who are sent to her! how many times I longed to gather your children together, as a hen which gathers her chickens under her wings, but you were not willing!
13.35: Behold, your house is left to you desolate; for I say to you, that you will not see me until you say, Blessed is he who comes in the name of the Lord.

14.1: AND it came to pass when he entered the house of one of the leaders of the Pharisees to eat bread on a sabbath day, they watched him.
14.2: And there was a man before him, who had dropsy.
14.3: And Jesus answered and said to the scribes and Pharisees, Is it lawful to heal on the sabbath?
14.4: But they kept silent. So he took him, and healed him, and let him go.
14.5: And he said to them, Which one of you, if his son or his ox should fall into a pit on the sabbath day, would not immediately pull and bring him out?
14.6: And they could not answer him concerning this.
14.7: And he spoke a parable to those who were invited there, because he saw them choosing places among the front seats.
14.8: When you are invited of a man to a banquet house, do not go and sit in the front seat; it might be that a more honorable man than you is invited there;
14.9: And then he who has invited you and him will come, and say to you, Give the place to him; and you will be embarrassed when you get up and take a lower seat.
14.10: But when you are invited, go and sit at the lower end, so that when he who has invited you comes, he will say to you, My friend, go up and sit higher; and you will have glory before all who sit with you.
14.11: For whoever exalts himself will be humbled; and whoever humbles himself will be exalted.
14.12: He also said to him who had invited him, When you give a dinner or a supper, do not invite your friends, nor your brothers, nor your relatives, nor your rich neighbors; they might probally invite you, and you will be repaid for this.
14.13: But when you give a reception, invite the poor, the maimed, the lame and the blind;
14.14: And you will be blessed; for they have nothing to repay you; for you will be repaid at the resurrection of the righteous.
14.15: When one of the guests heard these things, he said to him, Blessed is he who will eat bread in the kingdom of God.
14.16: Jesus said to him, A man gave a great supper, and invited many.
14.17: And he sent his servant at supper time to tell those who were invited, Behold, everything is made ready for you, come.
14.18: One and all, they began to make excuse. The first said to him, I have bought a field, and I am forced to go and see it; I beg you to excuse me for being called away.
14.19: Another said, I have bought five yoke of oxen, and I am just going to examine them; I beg you, excuse me for being called away.
14.20: Another said, I have just taken a wife, and therefore I cannot come.
14.21: And the servant came and told his master these things. Then the master of the house was angry, and said to his servant, Go out quickly to the streets and lanes of the city, and bring in here the poor, the afflicted, the maimed and the blind.
14.22: And the servant said, My Lord, it has been done as you commanded, and yet there is more room.
14.23: Then the master said to his servant, Go out to the highways and hedges, and urge them to come in so that my house may be filled.
14.24: For I say to you, that not one of those men who were invited shall taste of my supper.
14.25: And while many people were going with him, he turned and said to them,
14.26: He who comes to me and does not put aside his father, and his mother, and his brothers, and his sisters, and his wife, and his children, and even his own life, he cannot be a disciple to me.
14.27: And he who does not take up his cross and follow me, cannot be a disciple to me.
14.28: For which of you, who wishes to build a tower, does not at first sit down and consider its cost, to see if he has enough to finish it?
14.29: Lest after he has laid the foundation, he is not able to finish it, and all who see it will mock him,
14.30: Saying, This man began to build, but he was not able to finish.
14.31: Or which king, who goes to war to fight against a king equal to him, would not at first reason, whether he is able with ten thousand to meet the one who is coming against him with twenty thousand?
14.32: And if not, while he is far away from him, sends envoys and seeks peace.
14.33: So every man of you, who would not leave all his possessions, cannot be a disciple to me.
14.34: Salt is good; but if the salt lose its savor, with what can it be salted?
14.35: It is good neither for the ground nor for fertilizing; but it is thrown out. He who has ears to hear let him hear.

15.1: THEN the publicans and sinners drew near to him to hear him.
15.2: And the scribes and Pharisees murmured, saying, He receives even the sinners and eats with them.
15.3: So Jesus told them this parable,
15.4: What man among you has one hundred sheep, and if one of them should get lost, would he not leave the ninety and nine in the open, and go in search of the one which is lost, until he finds it?
15.5: And when he finds it he rejoices, and he takes it on his shoulders.
15.6: And he comes to his house, and invites his friends and neighbors, and says to them, Rejoice with me, for I have found my sheep which was lost.
15.7: I say to you, that such will be the joy in heaven over one sinner who repents, than over ninety and nine righteous, who need no repentance.
15.8: Or what woman who has ten coins, and should lose one of them, would not light a lamp and sweep the house, and search for it carefully, until she finds it?
15.9: And when she finds it, she calls her women friends and neighbors, and says to them, Rejoice with me, for I have found my coin which was lost.
15.10: I say to you, that such will be the joy before the angels of God over one sinner who repents.
15.11: And Jesus said to them again, A man had two sons;
15.12: And his younger son said to him, My father, give me the portion, which is coming to me from your house. And he divided to them his possessions.
15.13: And after a few days, his younger son gathered everything that was his share, and went to a far-away country, and there he scattered his wealth in extravagant living.
15.14: And when all he had was gone, there was a severe famine in that country; and he began to be in need.
15.15: So he went and got acquainted with one of the men of the city of that country; and he sent him to the field to feed the swine.
15.16: And he craved to fill his stomach with the husks that the swine were eating; and yet no man would give him.
15.17: And when he came to himself, he said, How many hired workers are now in my father’s house, who have plenty of bread, and I am here perishing with hunger!
15.18: I will rise and go to my father, and say to him, My father, I have sinned before heaven, and before you;
15.19: And I am no longer worthy to be called your son; just make me like one of your hired workers.
15.20: And he rose up and came to his father. And while he was yet at a distance, his father saw him, and had compassion on him, and he ran and fell on his neck and kissed him.
15.21: And his son said to him, My father, I have sinned before heaven and before you, and I am not worthy to be called your son.
15.22: But his father said to his servants, Bring the best robe and put it on him, and put a ring on his hand, and shoes on his feet;
15.23: And bring and kill the fat ox, and let us eat and be merry;
15.24: For this my son was dead, and has come to life; he was lost and is found. And they began to be merry.
15.25: But his elder son was in the field; and as he came near the house, he heard the voice of the singing of many.
15.26: And he called one of the boys, and asked him what it was all about.
15.27: He said to him, Your brother has come; and your father has killed the fat ox, because he received him safe and well.
15.28: And he became angry and would not go in; so his father came out and besought him.
15.29: But he said to his father, Behold, how many years I have served you, and I never disobeyed your commandment; and yet you never gave me even a kid, that I might make merry with my friends.
15.30: But for this son of yours, after he had wasted your wealth with harlots and come back, you have killed the fat ox.
15.31: His father said to him, My son, you are always with me, and everything which is mine is yours.
15.32: It was right for us to make merry and rejoice; for this your brother was dead and has come to life; and was lost and is found.

16.1: AND he spoke a parable to his dis ciples, There was a rich man, who had a steward; and they accused him that he was wasting his wealth.
16.2: So his master called him and said to him, What is this that I hear concerning you? Give me an account of your stewardship; for no longer can you be a steward for me.
16.3: Then the steward said to himself, What will I do? for my lord will take away from me the stewardship? I cannot dig, and I am ashamed to beg.
16.4: Now I know what I will do, so that when I leave the stewardship, they will receive me in their houses.
16.5: And he called his lord’s debtors, one by one, and said to the first, How much do you owe my lord?
16.6: He said to him, A hundred pounds of butter. He said to him, Take your note, sit down quickly, and write fifty pounds.
16.7: And he said to another, And you, what do you owe to my lord? He said to him, One hundred bushels of wheat. He said to him, Take your note, and sit down and write eighty bushels.
16.8: And the lord praised the unjust steward because he had done wisely; for the children of this world are wiser in their generation than the children of light.
16.9: And I also say, use this earthly wealth, however acquired, to make friends so that when it is gone, they will receive you and you will have everlasting habitation.
16.10: He who is faithful with little, is also faithful with much; and he who is dishonest with little, is also dishonest with much.
16.11: If therefore, you are not faithful with the wealth of iniquity, who will believe that there is any truth in you?
16.12: And if you are not found faithful with that which is not your own, who will give you that which is your own?
16.13: No servant can serve two masters; for either he will hate the one and like the other; or he will honor one and despise the other. You cannot serve God and mammon (wealth).
16.14: When the Pharisees heard all these things, because they loved money, they ridiculed him.
16.15: But Jesus said to them, You are the ones who make yourselves righteous before men; but God knows your hearts. For what is highly esteemed among men is disgusting in the presence of God.
16.16: The law and prophets were until John; from that time the kingdom of God is preached, and everyone presses to enter into it.
16.17: It is easier for heaven and earth to pass away than for one letter of the law to pass away.
16.18: He who divorces his wife and marries another commits adultery; and he who marries the one who is separated commits adultery.
16.19: There was a rich man, who used to wear purple and fine linen, and every day he made merry very lavishly.
16.20: And there was a poor man named Lazarus, who was laid down at that rich man’s door, afflicted with boils;
16.21: He longed to fill his stomach with the crumbs that fell from the rich man’s tray; the dogs also came and licked his boils.
16.22: Now it happened that the poor man died, and the angels carried him into Abraham’s bosom; and the rich man also died and was buried.
16.23: And while he was tormented in Sheol, he lifted up his eyes from a distance, and saw Abraham, with Lazarus in his bosom.
16.24: And he called in a loud voice and said, O my father Abraham, have mercy on me, and send Lazarus to dip his finger in water and wet my tongue; for I am tormented in this flame.
16.25: Abraham said to him, My son, remember you received your pleasures when you were living, and Lazarus his hardships; and behold now he is comfortable here, and you are suffering.
16.26: Besides all these things, a great gulf is fixed between us and you; so that those who wish to cross over from here to you cannot, neither from there to cross over to us.
16.27: He said to him, If that is so, I beseech you, O my father, to send him to my father’s house;
16.28: For I have five brothers; let him go and testify to them, so that they may not also come to this place of torment.
16.29: Abraham said to him, They have Moses and the prophets; let them hear them.
16.30: But he said to him, No, my father Abraham; but if only a man from the dead go to them, they will repent.
16.31: Abraham said to him, If they will not hear Moses and the prophets, neither will they believe even if a man should rise from the dead.

17.1: AND Jesus said to his disciples, It is impossible but that offences should come; but woe to him by whose hand they come!
17.2: It were better for him that an ass’ millstone were hanged on his neck, and he thrown into the sea, than cause one of these little ones to stumble.
17.3: Beware among yourselves. If your brother should sin, rebuke him; and if he repents, forgive him.
17.4: And if he should offend you seven times in a day, and seven times in a day turn to you and say, I repent; forgive him.
17.5: And the apostles said to our Lord, Increase our faith.
17.6: He said to them, If you have faith, even as a grain of mustard seed, you could say to this mulberry tree, Be uprooted and planted in the sea; it would obey you.
17.7: Now which of you has a servant who ploughs or feeds sheep, and if he should come from the field, would say to him, Enter in and sit down?
17.8: But he will rather say to him, Prepare something that I may have my supper, and gird yourself and serve me until I eat and drink; and then you also can eat and drink.
17.9: Why, will that servant receive praise, because he did what he was commanded to do? I do not think so.
17.10: Even you also, when you have done all the things which are commanded you, say, We are idle servants; we have only done what was our duty to do.
17.11: And it came to pass, while Jesus was going to Jerusalem, he passed through Samaritan territory which is towards Galilee.
17.12: And when he drew near to enter a village, he was met by ten lepers, and they stood afar off;
17.13: And they lifted their voices saying, O Jesus, our Master, have mercy on us.
17.14: And when he saw them he said to them, Go, show yourselves to the priests; and while they were going, they were cleansed.
17.15: But one of them, when he saw that he was cleansed, turned back, and with a loud voice praised God.
17.16: And he fell on his face at the feet of Jesus, thanking him; and this one was a Samaritan.
17.17: Jesus answered and said, Were there not ten who were cleansed? where are the nine?
17.18: Why did they separate themselves so as not to come and give praise to God, except this man who is of a strange people?
17.19: And he said to him, Arise, go, your faith has healed you.
17.20: When some of the Pharisees asked Jesus, when the kingdom of God would come, he answered and said to them, The kingdom of God does not come by observation.
17.21: Neither will they say, Behold, it is here! or, behold, it is there! for behold, the kingdom of God is within you.
17.22: And he said to his disciples, The days will come when you will covet to see one of the days of the Son of man, and you will not see it.
17.23: And if they should say to you, Behold, he is here! and behold, he is there! do not go.
17.24: For just as the lightning flashes from the sky, and all under the sky is lightened, such will be the day of the Son of man.
17.25: But first he must suffer a great many things, and be rejected by this generation.
17.26: Just as it happened in the days of Noah, such will it be in the days of the Son of man.
17.27: For they were eating and drinking, and marrying women, and giving in marriage, until the day when Noah entered the ark, and the flood came, and destroyed every man.
17.28: And again, just as it happened in the days of Lot; they were eating and drinking, and buying and selling, and planting and building;
17.29: But in the day when Lot went out of Sodom, the Lord sent down a rain of fire and sulphur from heaven, and destroyed them all.
17.30: Such will it be in the day when the Son of man appears.
17.31: In that day, he who is on the roof and his clothes in the house, will not come down to take them; and he who is in the field will not return back.
17.33: He who desires to save his life shall lose it; and he who loses his life shall save it.
17.34: I say to you that in that very night two will be in one bed; one will be taken away, and the other left.
17.35: And two women will be grinding together; one will be taken away, and the other left.
17.36: Two will be in the field; one will be taken, and the other left.
17.37: They answered and said to him, Our Lord, to what place? He said to them, Wherever the corpse is, there will the eagles gather.

18.1: HE also spake to them a parable, that they should pray always and not get weary.
18.2: There was a judge in a city, who did not fear God, and had no regard for men.
18.3: There was a widow in that city, and she used to come to him, saying, Avenge me of my accuser.
18.4: And he would not for a long time; but afterwards he said within himself, Though I am not afraid of God, and have no regard for men;
18.5: Yet because this widow troubles me, I will avenge her, so that she may not keep coming and annoy me.
18.6: Then our Lord said, Hear what the unjust judge said.
18.7: Would not God avenge his chosen ones much more, who call upon him day and night, though he has patience with them?
18.8: I say to you, he will avenge them promptly. But when the Son of man comes, will he find faith on the earth?
18.9: And he said this parable against the men who relied upon themselves that they were righteous, and despised every man.
18.10: Two men went up to the temple to pray; one a Pharisee, and the other a publican.
18.11: And the Pharisee stood by himself, and prayed thus: O God, I thank thee, that I am not like the rest of men, extortioners, grafters, adulterers, and not like this publican.
18.12: But I fast twice a week, and I give tithes on everything I earn.
18.13: But the publican stood afar off, and he would not even lift up his eyes to heaven, but smote his breast, saying, O God, be merciful to me, I am a sinner.
18.14: I say to you, that this man went down to his house more righteous than the Pharisee. For everyone who exalts himself will be humbled; and everyone who humbles himself will be exalted.
18.15: They brought to him also little boys, that he might touch them; and his disciples saw them and rebuked them.
18.16: But Jesus called them, and said to them, Permit the children to come to me, and do not stop them; for the kingdom of heaven is for those who are like these.
18.17: Truly I say to you, He who will not receive the kingdom of God like a little boy will never enter into it.
18.18: A nd one of the leaders asked him and said to him, O good Teacher, what shall I do to inherit life everlasting?
18.19: Jesus said to him, Why do you call me good? there is no one good, except one, that is God.
18.20: You know the commandments: You shall not kill; You shall not commit adultery; You shall not steal; You shall not bear false witness; Honor your father and your mother.
18.21: He said to him, All these I have obeyed from my boyhood.
18.22: When Jesus heard it, he said to him, You lack one thing; go, sell everything you have, and give it to the poor, and you will have a treasure in heaven; and come and follow me.
18.23: But when he heard these things, he felt sad, because he was very rich.
18.24: And when Jesus saw that he felt sad, he said, How difficult it is for those who have wealth to enter into the kingdom of God!
18.25: It is easier for a rope to go through the eye of a needle, than for a rich man to enter into the kingdom of God.
18.26: Those who heard it said to him, Who then can be saved?
18.27: But Jesus said, Those things which are impossible to men are possible to God.
18.28: Simon Peter said to him, Behold we have left everything and followed you.
18.29: Jesus said to him, Truly I say to you, that there is no man who leaves houses, or parents, or brothers, or wife, or children for the sake of the kingdom of God,
18.30: Who will not receive many times more at this time, and in the world to come life everlasting.
18.31: Then Jesus took the twelve and said to them, Behold, we are going up to Jerusalem, and all things which are written by the prophets concerning the Son of man will be fulfilled.
18.32: For he will be delivered to the Gentiles, and they will mock him, and spit in his face.
18.33: And they will scourge him, and curse him, and kill him; and on the third day he will rise again.
18.34: But they understood not one of these things; and this saying was hidden from them, and they did not know these things which were spoken to them.
18.35: And when he drew near Jericho, a blind man was sitting by the roadside and begging.
18.36: And he heard the voice of the people passing, and asked, Who is this?
18.37: They said to him, Jesus the Nazarene is passing.
18.38: And he cried and said, O Jesus, son of David, have mercy on me.
18.39: And those who were going before Jesus rebuked him, to keep quiet; but he cried the more, O son of David, have mercy on me.
18.40: So Jesus stood still, and commanded to call him to him; and as he came near him he asked him,
18.41: And said to him, What do you wish me to do for you? He answered and said, My Lord, that I may see.
18.42: And Jesus said to him, See; your faith has healed you.
18.43: And he saw immediately, and followed him, and praised God; and all the people who saw it, gave praise to God.

19.1: AND when Jesus entered and passed through Jericho,
19.2: There was a man named Zacchaeus, who was rich and chief of the publicans.
19.3: And he wanted to see who Jesus was; but he could not because of the crowd, for Zacchaeus was small in his stature.
19.4: So he ran ahead of Jesus, and climbed up into a fig tree without leaves, that he might see him, because he was to pass that way.
19.5: When Jesus came to that place, he saw him and said to him, Make haste, come down, O Zacchaeus, for today I must remain in your house.
19.6: And he hastened, and came down, and welcomed him with joy.
19.7: Now when they all saw it, they murmured, saying, He has entered to stay in the house of a sinner.
19.8: But Zacchaeus rose up and said to Jesus, Behold, my Lord, half of my wealth I will give to the poor; and I will pay fourfold to every man from whom I have extorted.
19.9: Jesus said to him, Today life has come to this house, because he also is a son of Abraham.
19.10: For the Son of man came to seek and save that which was lost.
19.11: While they were listening to these things, he added and spoke a parable, because he was near Jerusalem, and they were expecting that the kingdom of God would appear at that very hour;
19.12: And he said, A great man of a noble family went to a far country to receive for himself a kingdom, and return.
19.13: And he called his ten servants, and gave them ten pounds, and said to them, Do business until I come back.
19.14: But the people of his city hated him, and sent messengers after him, saying, We do not want him to rule over us.
19.15: And when he received the kingdom and returned, he commanded to call his servants, to whom he had given the money, that he might know what each one of them had gained in business.
19.16: The first one came and said, My lord, your pound has gained ten pounds.
19.17: He said to him, O good servant, because you are found faithful in a little, you will have charge over ten talents.
19.18: And the second came and said, My lord, your pound has gained five pounds.
19.19: He said to this one also, You also will have charge over five talents.
19.20: And another one came and said, My lord, here is your pound which was with me, which I kept laid up in a purse.
19.21: For I was afraid of you, because you are a harsh man; you pick up what you have not laid down, and you reap what you have not sown.
19.22: He said to him, I will judge you from your own mouth, O wicked servant. You knew me that I am a harsh man, and pick up what I have not laid down, and reap what I have not sown.
19.23: Why then did you not give my money to the exchange, so that when I came I could demand it with its interest?
19.24: And he said to those who stood in his presence, Take away the pound from him, and give it to him who has ten pounds.
19.25: They said to him, Our lord, he has already with him ten pounds.
19.26: He said to them, I say to you, to everyone who has shall be given; and from him who has not, even that which he has will be taken away from him.
19.27: But those my enemies, who were not willing that I should rule over them, bring them here, and kill them before me.
19.28: And when Jesus had said these things, he went forward to go to Jerusalem.
19.29: And when he arrived at Bethphage and Bethany, on the side of the mountain which is called the Home of Olives, he sent two of his disciples,
19.30: And he said to them, Go to the village which is in front of us; and when you enter it you will find a colt tied up, on which no man has ever ridden; untie it and bring it.
19.31: And if any man should ask you, Why do you untie it? tell him this: Our lord needs it.
19.32: And those who were sent went away, and found just as he had told them.
19.33: And as they were untying the colt, its owners said to them, Why do you untie the colt?
19.34: And they said to them, Our Lord needs it.
19.35: And they brought it to Jesus; and they put their garments on the colt, and they set Jesus on it.
19.36: And as he went on, they spread their garments on the road.
19.37: And when he came near to the descent of the Mount of the Home of Olives, the whole multitude of the disciples began to rejoice, praising God with a loud voice, for all the miracles which they had seen,
19.38: Saying, Blessed is the king who comes in the name of the Lord; peace in heaven and glory in the highest.
19.39: But some of the men of the Pharisees who were in the multitude said to him, Teacher, rebuke your disciples.
19.40: He said to them, I say to you, that if these should keep silent, the stones would cry out.
19.41: And when he drew near and saw the city, he wept over it;
19.42: And he said, If you had only known those who are concerned in your peace, even in this your day! but now they are hidden from your eyes.
19.43: But the days will come to you, when your enemies will surround you, and oppress you from every place,
19.44: And will overthrow you, and your children within you; and they will not leave in you a stone upon a stone; because you did not know the time when you were to be visited.
19.45: And when he entered the temple, he began to put out those who were buying and selling in it;
19.46: And he said to them, It is written, My house is the house of prayer; but you have made it a cave of bandits.
19.47: And he taught every day in the temple. But the high priests and the scribes and the elders of the people sought to get rid of him;
19.48: But they were not able to find what to do to him; for all the people hung around him to hear him.

20.1: AND it came to pass on one of the days, while he was teaching the people and preaching in the temple, the high priests and the scribes with the elders rose up against him.
20.2: And they said to him, Tell us by what authority you do these things, and who gave you this authority?
20.3: Jesus answered and said to them, I will also ask you a word, and you tell me;
20.4: The baptism of John, was it from heaven or from men?
20.5: And they reasoned with themselves, saying, If we should say from heaven, he will say to us, Why then did you not believe him?
20.6: And if we should say from men, all the people will stone us; for they regard John as a prophet.
20.7: So they said to him, We do not know whence it is.
20.8: Jesus said to them, Neither will I tell you by what authority I do these things.
20.9: And he began to say this parable to the people, A man planted a vineyard, and leased it to laborers, and went on a journey for a long time.
20.10: And at the season he sent his servant to the laborers to give him of the fruit of the vineyard; but the laborers beat him and sent him back empty.
20.11: And again he sent another of his servants; but they beat him also, and treated him shamefully, and sent him back empty.
20.12: And again he sent the third one; but they wounded him also, and threw him outside.
20.13: Then the owner of the vineyard said, What shall I do? I will send my beloved son; perhaps they will see him and feel ashamed.
20.14: But when the laborers saw him, they reasoned with themselves, saying, This is the heir; come, let us kill him, and the inheritance will be ours.
20.15: So they cast him out of the vineyard, and killed him. What therefore will the owner of the vineyard do to them?
20.16: He will come and destroy those laborers, and give the vineyard to others. And when they heard it, they said, This will never happen.
20.17: But he looked at them and said, What is it that is written, The stone which the builders rejected, the same became the cornerstone?
20.18: Whoever falls on that stone will be broken; and on whomever it falls it will scatter him.
20.19: The high priests and the scribes sought to lay hands on him that very hour; but they were afraid of the people; for they knew that he had spoken this parable against them.
20.20: So they sent spies disguised as righteous men, to ensnare him by a word, and to deliver him to the judge, and then to the authority of the governor.
20.21: So they asked him, and said to him, Teacher, we know that you speak and teach truthfully, and you do not discriminate be- tween men, but you teach the way of God justly.
20.22: Is it lawful for us to pay head-tax to Caesar or not?
20.23: But he understood their craftiness and said, Why do you tempt me?
20.24: Show me a penny. Whose image and inscription are on it? They said, Caesar’s.
20.25: Jesus said to them, Give therefore to Caesar that which is Caesar’s and to God what is God’s.
20.26: And they were not able to get a word from him before the people; and they were amazed at his answer, and kept silence.
20.27: Then came to him some of the men of the Sadducees, those who say there is no resurrection; and they asked him,
20.28: Teacher, Moses wrote to us, that if a man’s brother should die, and has a wife without children, let his brother take his wife and raise up offspring for his brother.
20.29: Now there were seven brothers; the first married and died without children.
20.30: The second married his wife, and he died without children.
20.31: And the third one married her again; and likewise the seven of them; and they died leaving no children.
20.32: And at last the woman also died.
20.33: Therefore at the resurrection to which one of them will she be a wife? for seven of them married her.
20.34: Jesus said to them, The sons of this world marry women, and women are given to men in marriage.
20.35: But those who are worthy of the other world, and the resurrection from the dead, neither take women in marriage nor are women given in marriage to men.
20.36: For they cannot die again, because they are like angels; and they are sons of God, because they are sons of the resurrection.
20.37: Now concerning the resurrection of the dead, even Moses pointed out; for he referred to it at the Bush when he said, The Lord God of Abraham, and the God of Isaac, and the God of Jacob.
20.38: God is not the God of the dead but of the living; for all live to him.
20.39: And some of the men of the scribes answered and said to him, Teacher, you have well said.
20.40: And they did not dare again to question him concerning anything.
20.41: And he said to them, How can the scribes say concerning the Christ, that he is son of David?
20.42: And yet David said in the book of Psalms, The Lord said to my Lord, Sit at my right hand,
20.43: Until I put down your enemies under your feet.
20.44: If, therefore David calls him my Lord, how then can he be his son?
20.45: And while all the people were listening, he said to his disciples,
20.46: Beware of the scribes, who like to walk in long robes, and love to be greeted in the streets, and the chief seats in the synagogues, and the high places at the banquets;
20.47: Those who embezzle the property of widows with the pretence that they make long prayers; they will receive a greater judgment.

21.1: JESUS then looked at the rich men who were casting their offerings into the trea sury.
21.2: And he also saw a poor widow, who cast in two pennies.
21.3: And he said, Truly I say to you, that this poor widow has cast in more than every man.
21.4: For all these cast into the house of the offerings of God of their abundance; but she of her poverty cast in everything she had earned.
21.5: While some men were talking about the temple, that it was adorned with beautiful stones and gift offerings, Jesus said to them,
21.6: These things which you see, the days will come when not a stone will be left upon a stone, which will not be torn down.
21.7: And they asked him, saying, Teacher, when will these things happen? and what is the sign when these things are about to happen?
21.8: He said to them, Be careful that you may not be deceived; for many will come in my name, and say, I am the Christ; and the time is near; but do not follow them.
21.9: And when you hear of wars and revolutions, do not be afraid; for all these things must first come to pass; but the end is not vet.
21.10: For nation will rise against nation, and kingdom against kingdom.
21.11: And there will be great earthquakes in different places, and famines and plagues; and there will be alarming sights, and great signs will appear from heaven; and the winters will be severe.
21.12: But before all these things, they will lay hands on you, and persecute you, and deliver you to the synagogues and the prisons; and they will bring you before kings and governors for the sake of my name.
21.13: It will be to you for a testimony.
21.14: Treasure it in your heart, and do not try to learn what to answer.
21.15: For I will give you a mouth and wisdom, which all your enemies will not be able to withstand.
21.16: You will be delivered up even by your parents and brothers, and your relatives and friends; and they will put some of you to death.
21.17: And you will be hated by every man because of my name.
21.18: And yet not a hair of your head will be lost.
21.19: By your patience you will gain your souls.
21.20: But when you see Jerusalem surrounded by an army, then know that its destruction is at hand.
21.21: Then let those who are in Judaea flee to the mountain; and let those who are within it flee; and let those who are in the fields not enter into it.
21.22: For these are the days of vengeance, so that everything which is written must be fulfilled.
21.23: But woe to those who are with child, and to those who give suck in those days! For there will be great distress in the land, and wrath to this people.
21.24: And they will fall by the edge of the sword, and they will be taken captive to every country; and Jerusalem will be trodden under the feet of the Gentiles, until the time of the Gentiles comes to an end.
21.25: And there will be signs in sun and moon and stars; and on earth distress of the nations, and confusion because of the roaring of the sea;
21.26: And upheaval that takes life out of men, because of fear of what is to come on earth; and the powers of the universe will be shaken.
21.27: Then they will see the Son of man coming in the clouds, with a large army and great glory.
21.28: But when these things begin to happen, have courage, and lift up your heads; because your salvation is at hand.
21.29: And he said to them a parable, Look at the fig tree and all the trees;
21.30: When they put forth leaves, you immediately understand by them that the summer is near.
21.31: Even so you also, when you see these things happen, know that the kingdom of God is near.
21.32: Truly I say to you, This generation will not pass away until all these things happen.
21.33: Heaven and earth will pass away, but my words shall not pass away.
21.34: But take heed to yourselves, that your hearts may not become heavy by extravagance, and drunkenness, and worries of this world, and that day come suddenly upon you.
21.35: For like a downpour it will entrap all those who dwell on the face of all the earth.
21.36: Therefore keep watch all the time and pray, so that you may be worthy to escape all these things which are to happen, and that you may stand before the Son of man.
21.37: During the day he taught in the temple; and at night he went out, and lodged in the mountain, which is called the Home of Olives.
21.38: And all the people came ahead of him to the temple, to hear him.

22.1: NOW the feast of unleavened bread, which is called the passover, was at hand.
22.2: And the high priests and the scribes sought how to kill him; but they were afraid of the people.
22.3: But Satan had taken possession of Judas who is called of Iscariot, who was of the number of the twelve.
22.4: So he went away, and spoke with the high priests and the scribes and officers of the temple, about delivering him to them.
22.5: And they were glad and promised to give him money.
22.6: And he agreed with them, and sought an opportunity to deliver him to them in the absence of the people.
22.7: Then the day of unleavened bread came, on which it was the custom to kill the passover lamb.
22.8: So Jesus sent Peter and John, and said to them, Go and prepare the passover for us to eat.
22.9: They said to him, Where do you wish us to prepare?
22.10: He said to them, Behold, when you enter the city, you will meet a man carrying a skin full of water; follow him. And wherever he enters,
22.11: Say to the master of the house, Our Teacher says, Where is the guest room, where I may eat the passover with my disciples?
22.12: And behold, he will show you an upper room, large and furnished; there make ready.
22.13: And they went and found just as he had said to them; and they prepared the passover.
22.14: And when the time came, Jesus came and sat down, and the twelve apostles with him.
22.15: And he said to them, I have desired with desire to eat this passover with you before I suffer;
22.16: For I say to you, that henceforth, I will not eat it, until it is fulfilled in the kingdom of God.
22.17: And he took the cup and gave thanks and said, Take this, and divide it among yourselves.
22.18: For I say to you, I will not drink of the fruit of the vine until the kingdom of God comes.
22.19: And he took bread and gave thanks and broke it, and gave it to them and said, This is my body, which is given for your sake; this do in remembrance of me.
22.20: And likewise also the cup, after they had eaten the supper, he said, This is the cup of the new covenant in my blood, which is shed for you.
22.21: But behold, the hand of him who is to betray me is on the table.
22.22: And the Son of man will go, just as he has been destined; but woe to the man by whose hand he will be betrayed!
22.23: And they began to enquire among themselves, which one of them was to do this act.
22.24: There was also a dispute among them, as to who is the greatest among them.
22.25: Jesus said to them, The kings of the Gentiles are also their owners; and those who rule over them are called benefactors.
22.26: But not so with you; but he who is great among you, let him be the least, and he who is a leader be like a minister.
22.27: For who is greater, he who sits down or he who serves? Is it not he who sits down? but I am among you as one who serves.
22.28: You are those who have remained with me, throughout my trials.
22.29: And I promise you, just as my Father has promised me, a kingdom,
22.30: That you may eat and drink at the table in my kingdom; and you will sit on chairs, and judge the twelve tribes of Israel.
22.31: And Jesus said to Simon, Simon, behold Satan wants to sift all of you as wheat;
22.32: But I have made supplication for you that your faith may not weaken; and even you in time will repent, and strengthen your brethren.
22.33: Simon said to him, My Lord, I am ready with you, even for the prison and for death.
22.34: Jesus said to him, I say to you, Simon, the cock will not crow today, until you have denied three times that you know me.
22.35: And he said to them, When I sent you out without purses, and without bags, and shoes, did you lack anything? They said to him, Not a thing.
22.36: He said to them, From now on he who has purses, let him take them, and the bag likewise; and he who has no sword, let him sell his robe and buy for himself a sword.
22.37: For I say to you, that this which is written must be fulfilled in me, He will be numbered among the wicked; for all the things concerning me will be fulfilled.
22.38: And they said to him, Our Lord, behold here are two swords. He said to them, That is enough.
22.39: And he went out and went away, as it was his custom, to the Mount of the Home of Olives; and his disciples also followed him.
22.40: And when he arrived at a place, he said to them, Pray that you may not enter into temptation.
22.41: And he separated from them, about the distance of a stone’s throw, and he kneeled down, and prayed,
22.42: Saying, O Father, if you will, let this cup pass from me; but not as I will, but your will be done.
22.43: And there appeared to him an angel from heaven, to strengthen him.
22.44: And he was in fear, and prayed earnestly; and his sweat became like drops of blood; and he fell down upon the ground.
22.45: Then he rose up from his prayer, and came to his disciples, and found them sleeping because of distress.
22.46: And he said to them, Why do you sleep? rise and pray that you may not enter into temptation.
22.47: While he was still speaking, behold, a multitude, and he who is called Judas, one of the twelve, coming before them; and he drew near to Jesus and kissed him. For this was the sign he had given them, He whom I kiss, it is he.
22.48: Jesus said to him, Judas, do you betray the Son of man with a kiss?
22.49: When those who were with him saw what happened, they said to him, Our Lord, shall we smite them with swords?
22.50: And one of them struck the servant of the high priest, and cut off his right ear.
22.51: But Jesus answered and said, It is enough for the present. And he touched the ear of him who was wounded, and healed it.
22.52: And Jesus said to the high priests and the elders and the officers of the temple, who had come against him, Have you come out against me to arrest me with swords and staves, as if you were against a bandit?
22.53: I was with you every day in the temple, and you did not even point your hands at me; but this is your turn, and the power of darkness.
22.54: And they arrested him, and brought him to the house of the high priest. And Simon followed him afar off.
22.55: And they kindled a fire in the midst of the courtyard, and they sat around it; and Simon also sat among them.
22.56: And a young woman saw him sitting by the fire, and she looked at him and said, This man also was with him.
22.57: But he denied, and said, Woman, I do not know him.
22.58: And after a little while, another saw him, and said to him, You also are one of them. But Peter said, I am not.
22.59: And after an hour, another one argued and said, Truly, this man also was with him; for he is also a Galilean.
22.60: Peter said, Man, I do not know what you are saying. And immediately while he was still speaking, the cock crew.
22.61: And Jesus turned and looked at Peter. And Simon remembered the word of our Lord, that he said to him, Before the cock crows you will deny me three times.
22.62: And Simon went outside and wept bitterly.
22.63: And the men who held Jesus mocked him,
22.64: And they covered his head, and smote him on his face, saying, Prophesy, who has struck you.
22.65: And many other things they blasphemed and said against him.
22.66: As soon as it was daybreak, the elders and the high priests and the scribes gathered together, and brought him up to their council chamber.
22.67: And they said to him, If you are the Christ, tell us. He said to them, If I tell you, you will not believe me.
22.68: And if I ask you, you will not answer me nor release me.
22.69: From henceforth the Son of man will sit at the right hand of the power of God.
22.70: And they all said, Are you then the Son of God? Jesus said to them, You say that I am.
22.71: And they said, Why then do we need witnesses? for we have heard it from his own mouth.

23.1: THEN the whole company of them rose up, and brought him to Pilate;
23.2: And began to accuse him, saying, We found this man misleading our people, and forbidding to pay the head-tax to Caesar; and he says concerning himself that he is a King, even the Christ.
23.3: Pilate asked him and said, Are you the king of the Jews? He said to him, You say that.
23.4: Then Pilate said to the high priests and the people, I cannot find any fault against this man.
23.5: But they shouted and said, He has stirred up our people, teaching throughout Judaea, and beginning from Galilee even to this place.
23.6: When Pilate heard the name Galilee, he asked if the man was a Galilean.
23.7: And when he knew that he was under the jurisdiction of Herod, he sent him to Herod, because he was in Jerusalem in those days.
23.8: When Herod saw Jesus he was exceedingly glad, for he had wanted to see him for a long time, because he had heard many things concerning him; and he hoped to see some miracle by him.
23.9: And he asked him many words; but Jesus gave him no answer.
23.10: But the high priests and the scribes stood, and accused him bitterly.
23.11: And Herod and his soldiers insulted him, and mocked him, and dressed him in a scarlet robe, and sent him to Pilate.
23.12: And that day Pilate and Herod became friends with each other; for there was a longstanding enmity between them.
23.13: Then Pilate called the high priests and the leaders of the people,
23.14: And he said to them, You brought me this man, as if he were misleading your people; and behold, I have examined him before your own eyes, and I have found no fault in this man concerning all that you accuse him.
23.15: Not even has Herod; for I sent him to him; and behold, he has done nothing worthy of death.
23.16: I will therefore chastise him, and release him.
23.17: For there was a custom to release to them one at the feast.
23.18: But all the people cried out saying, Get rid of him, and release to us Bar-Abbas;
23.19: Who because of sedition and murder which had happened in the city, was cast into prison.
23.20: Again Pilate spoke to them, desiring to release Jesus.
23.21: But they cried out, saying, Crucify him, crucify him.
23.22: And he said to them the third time, What evil has he done? I have found nothing in him, worthy of death; I will therefore chastise him, and release him.
23.23: But they persisted with loud voices, and asked to crucify him. And their voice and that of the high priests prevailed.
23.24: Then Pilate commanded to have their request granted.
23.25: So he released to them the one who because of sedition and murder was cast into prison, whom they asked for; and he delivered Jesus to their will.
23.26: And while they took him away, they laid hold of Simon, a Cyrenian, who was coming from the field, and they placed the end of the cross on him, to carry it with Jesus.
23.27: And many people followed him, and the women who were mourning and wailing over him.
23.28: But Jesus turned to them and said, O daughters of Jerusalem, do not weep over me; but weep over yourselves, and over your own children.
23.29: For behold, the days are coming, in which they will say, Blessed are the barren, and the wombs that never gave birth, and the breasts that never gave suck.
23.30: Then they will begin to say to the mountains, Fall on us; and to the hills, Cover us.
23.31: For if they do these things with the green wood, what will be done with dry wood?
23.32: And there were coming with him two others, malefactors, to be put to death.
23.33: And when they came to a place which is called The Skull, they crucified him there, and the malefactors, one on his right and one on his left.
23.34: And Jesus said, O Father, forgive them, for they know not what they are doing. And they divided his garments and cast lots over them.
23.35: The people stood looking on. And even the leaders of the synagogue mocked him, and said, He saved others; let him save himself, if he is the Christ, the chosen one of God.
23.36: And the soldiers ridiculed him, as they came near him and offered him vinegar,
23.37: Saying to him, If you are the king of the Jews, save yourself.
23.38: There was also an inscription which was written over him, in Greek and Roman, and Hebrew, THIS IS THE KING OF THE JEWS.
23.39: Now one of the malefactors who were crucified with him, blasphemed against him, saying, If you are the Christ, save yourself and save us also.
23.40: But the other rebuked him, and said to him, Do you not fear even God, for you are also in the same judgment?
23.41: And ours is just, for we are paid as we deserve and as we have done; but he has done nothing wrong.
23.42: And he said to Jesus, Remember me, my Lord, when you come in your kingdom.
23.43: Jesus said to him, Truly I say to you today, you will be with me in Paradise.
23.44: Now it was about the sixth hour, and darkness fell upon the whole earth, until the ninth hour.
23.45: And the sun was darkened, and the door curtains of the temple were torn in the center.
23.46: Then Jesus cried with a loud voice and said, O my Father, into thy hands I commit my spirit. He said this and passed away.
23.47: When the centurion saw what had happened, he praised God and said, Truly this was a righteous man.
23.48: And all the people who were gathered together to see this sight, when they saw what had happened, returned, beating their breasts.
23.49: And all the acquaintances of Jesus stood afar off, and the women who had come with him from Galilee, and they were beholding these things.
23.50: There was a man named Joseph the counsellor of Arimathaea, a city of Judaea, a good and righteous man.
23.51: He did not agree with their wishes and their actions; and he waited for the kingdom of God.
23.52: He went to Pilate and asked for the body of Jesus.
23.53: And he took it down and wrapped it in fine linen, and laid it in a hewn tomb, in which no one was ever laid.
23.54: This was a Friday, and the sabbath was approaching.
23.55: The women who had come with him from Galilee were near, and they saw the tomb, and how his body was laid.
23.56: And they returned and prepared spices and perfumes. And on the sabbath they rested, as it is commanded.

24.1: AND on the first day of the week, early in the morning, while it was yet dark, they came to the tomb, and brought the spices which they had prepared; and there were with them other women.
24.2: And they found the stone rolled away from the tomb.
24.3: And they entered in, but they did not find the body of Jesus.
24.4: And it came to pass as they were confused about this, behold, two men stood above them, and their garments were shining;
24.5: And they were afraid, and bowed their faces to the ground; and they said to them, Why do you seek the living among the dead?
24.6: He is not here, he has risen; remember that he spoke to you while he was in Galilee,
24.7: Saying, that the Son of man had to be delivered into the hands of sinful men,, and be crucified, and rise again on the third day.
24.9: And they returned from the tomb, and told all these things to the eleven and to the rest.
24.10: They were Mary of Magdala, and Joanna, and Mary the mother of James, and the rest who were with them, who told these things to the apostles.
24.11: And these words appeared in their eyes as delusions; and they did not believe them.
24.12: But Simon rose up and ran to the tomb; and he looked in and saw the linen laid by itself, and he went away wondering in himself concerning what had happened.
24.13: And behold two of them were going on that day to a village called Emmaus, about six miles from Jerusalem.
24.14: They were talking with each other concerning all these things that had happened.
24.15: And while they were speaking and asking each other, Jesus came and overtook them, and walked with them.
24.16: But the sight of their eyes was holden, so that they could not recognize him.
24.17: And he said to them, What are these words that you are discussing with each other, as you walk, and are sad?
24.18: One of them, named Cleopas, answered and said to him, Are you a stranger alone from Jerusalem, that you do not know what has happened in it in these days?
24.19: He said to them, What things? They said to him, About Jesus of Nazareth, a man who was a prophet, mighty in word and deed before God and before all the people.
24.20: And the high priests and the elders delivered him up to the judgment of death, and they crucified him.
24.21: But we were hoping that he was the one to save Israel; and behold, it is three days since all these things happened.
24.22: And some of our women also amazed us, for they went early to the tomb;
24.23: And when his body was not found, they came, and said to us, We saw angels there, and they said that he is alive.
24.24: And some of our men also went to the tomb, and they found it so, as the women had said; but they did not see him.
24.25: Then Jesus said to them, O dull-minded heavy-hearted, slow to believe all that the prophets have spoken.
24.26: Did not Christ have to suffer all these things, and to enter into his glory?
24.27: And he began from Moses and from all the prophets, and interpreted to them from all the scriptures concerning himself.
24.28: And they drew near to the village, to which they were going; and he made them think that he was going to a far place.
24.29: But they urged him and said, Remain with us; because the day is spent and it is near dark. So he entered to stay with them.
24.30: And it came to pass, as he sat at meat with them, he took bread and blessed it, and broke it, and gave it to them.
24.31: And immediately their eyes were opened and they recognized him; and he was taken away from them.
24.32: And they said one to another, Were not our hearts heavy within us, when he spoke with us on the road, and interpreted the scriptures to us?
24.33: And they rose up that very hour and returned to Jerusalem; and they found the eleven gathered together, and those who were with them,
24.34: Saying, Truly our Lord has risen, and he has appeared to Simon.
24.35: And they also reported those things that happened on the road, and how they knew him as he broke the bread.
24.36: And while they were discussing these things, Jesus stood among them, and said to them, Peace be with you; it is I; do not be afraid.
24.37: And they were confused and frightened, for they thought they saw a spirit. The walk to Emmaus.
24.38: Jesus said to them, Why do you tremble? and why do thoughts arise in your hearts?
24.39: Look at my hands and my feet, that it is I; feel me and understand; for a spirit has no flesh and bones, as you see I have.
24.40: When he said these things, he showed them his hands and his feet.
24.41: And as they still did not believe because of their joy, and they were bewildered, he said to them, Have you anything here to eat?
24.42: They gave him a portion of a broiled fish, and of a honeycomb.
24.43: And he took it, and ate before their eyes.
24.44: And he said to them, These are the words which I spoke to you when I was with you, that everything must be fulfilled which is written in the law of Moses, and in the prophets, and in the psalms, concerning me.
24.45: Then he opened their mind to understand the scriptures.
24.46: And he said to them, Thus it is written, and it was right that Christ should suffer, and rise from the dead on the third day;
24.47: And that repentance should be preached in his name for the forgiveness of sins among all nations; and the beginning will be from Jerusalem.
24.48: And you are witnesses of these things.
24.49: And I will send upon you the promise of my Father; but you remain in the city of Jerusalem, until you are clothed with power from on high.
24.50: And he took them out as far as Bethany, and he lifted up his hands and blessed them.
24.51: And it came to pass, while he blessed them, he parted from them, and went up to heaven.
24.52: And they worshipped him, and returned to Jerusalem with great joy;
24.53: And they were always in the temple,
